title: Assistant Buyer
description: Woodie's is the market\-leading DIY, Home \& Garden retailer in Ireland with 35 stores nationwide and an online presence. We are proud to be listed as Ireland's Top Retailer by Great Places to Work and an official Best Workplaces for Women for the past three years! We are also recognised by the Irish Centre for Diversity \& Inclusion as an accredited Investor in Diversity. **Job Purpose** Support the buying team in selecting, sourcing, and purchasing plant and outdoor ranges to meet customer needs and business targets. Interest in gardening and horticulture is highly beneficial. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in product selection, range expansion, and monitoring market trends. * Support supplier negotiations, maintain relationships, and ensure timely deliveries. * Help manage stock levels, product launches, and accurate records. * Monitor sales and stock, prepare reports, and recommend actions for underperforming products. * Mentor junior colleagues and provide administrative support. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to optimize buying processes. * Ensure compliance with company policies, ethical sourcing, and sustainability. **Skills \& Attributes** * Inventory Management: Proven experience in monitoring stock levels, managing product launches, and maintaining purchase records. * Commercial Administration: Strong background in supporting buying or merchandising teams with administrative tasks, sales and stock reporting, and supplier communications. * Understanding of gardening, horticulture, and seasonal demands. * Analytical, organised, and able to manage multiple tasks. * Strong communication and relationship\-building skills. * Results\-oriented, proactive, and professional. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at Woodie's! We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability status. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

title: Associate Accountant
description: **Role Overview** We are partnering with a growing Irish business to appoint an **Associate Accountant** to join its finance team in Dublin. This is a broad role with good exposure across month\-end, transactional finance, payroll, reporting, controls and systems. It would suit someone who enjoys a varied finance position, is comfortable working close to the numbers, and wants to build their career in a business that is continuing to scale. The successful candidate will work closely with senior finance colleagues and play an important role in keeping the finance function running smoothly while also supporting improvements to processes, reporting and controls. This is a good opportunity for someone who wants to develop in a hands\-on accounting role with exposure to both day\-to\-day finance and wider improvement projects. **Key Responsibilities** You will support across areas including: * Monthly accounts preparation and reporting * Balance sheet reconciliations and control account reviews * Variance analysis and regular management information * Accounts payable and receivable * Billing support and process improvement * Payroll processing and related reconciliations * Expense processing and policy compliance * Job costing and revenue recognition support * Audit preparation and year\-end work * Ad hoc analysis and broader finance team support There will also be an opportunity to contribute to finance improvement projects, including systems, controls, process consistency and reducing manual work across the function. **Candidate Profile** The ideal candidate will bring: * 2–5 years’ experience in a finance or accounting role * Part\-qualified accounting status, or strong relevant experience * Good grounding in core accounting principles * Experience with accounting systems, ideally Xero or a similar platform * Strong Excel skills * Good attention to detail and a reliable, organised approach * Confidence working in a busy, operational environment * A proactive attitude and willingness to take ownership * Strong communication skills and a collaborative working style

title: Automation Engineer
description: **Title:** Automation Engineer **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid) **Type:** 12\-month contract **Rate:** €70 \- €80 per hour **Ref:** DES\-1236 **Overview** Seeking an experienced Automation Engineer to join a Life Sciences delivery team supporting pharmaceutical and biotech projects across Ireland and Europe. The role focuses on developing and implementing automation solutions in regulated environments from design through to commissioning, working within multi\-disciplinary teams on complex project delivery. **Key Responsibilities** * Define and develop functional automation requirements (ISA S88\-based) * Support design, documentation, and delivery of automation systems * Provide technical input across design, build, and commissioning phases * Participate in workshops to refine scope and technical solutions * Liaise with engineers, contractors, and stakeholders across projects * Support FAT/SAT and resolve technical issues during commissioning * Ensure deliverables meet GMP, regulatory, and project standards * Guide and support junior engineers where required * Contribute to continuous improvement and project development **Requirements** * Degree in Engineering or related discipline * 10\+ years’ experience in controls/instrumentation engineering * Strong background in regulated pharma or biotech environments * Experience with GMP automation project delivery * Solid understanding of ISA S88 batch control principles * Strong communication and problem\-solving skills * Able to work independently and within project teams **Note:** Candidates must hold an EU passport or valid Irish work visa. For more information, contact Corné on 0949316002\.

title: Automation Engineer
description: 📍 South Dublin / North Wicklow \| 💼 Permanent \| 💶 Competitive DOE **🔧 AUTOMATION ENGINEER — MEDTECH MANUFACTURING** Are you a hands\-on Automation Engineer who is as comfortable talking to people as you are programming PLCs? We are working with a MedTech manufacturer based in South Dublin / North Wicklow who are looking for someone who can do more than just fix machines. They need someone who builds trust with the people around them, solves problems at pace and leaves relationships intact when they do it. This is a small, tight\-knit team where your work is visible and your contribution matters. **THE ROLE** You will work hands\-on across automation engineering, controls, maintenance and production support. Day to day you will cover a varied mix of fault finding, planned maintenance, system improvements and cross\-team collaboration. **Key responsibilities include but are not limited to the following:** • Programme and modify PLCs, HMIs and SCADA systems (Siemens, Allen\-Bradley and similar) • Install, configure and commission industrial automation hardware and software • Diagnose and resolve faults quickly, minimising production downtime • Perform routine and preventive maintenance on automation and control systems • Interpret electrical schematics, P\&IDs and technical drawings • Support system validation, testing and performance optimisation • Work closely with engineering, operations and maintenance teams daily • Maintain accurate documentation of system configurations and changes • Identify and implement improvements that increase reliability and productivity **WHAT YOU WILL NEED** • Degree or certification in Electrical Engineering, Mechatronics, Industrial Automation or related discipline • Minimum 2 years hands\-on experience with automation systems in a manufacturing or industrial environment • Strong PLC programming skills — Siemens, Allen\-Bradley or Schneider • HMI and SCADA configuration experience • Solid understanding of industrial control systems, sensors, instrumentation and safety standards • Ability to read and interpret electrical documentation confidently • Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving capability • Excellent communication skills — you will work with people at every level of the business **NICE TO HAVE** • PLC languages: Ladder Logic, Function Block, Structured Text • HMI/SCADA: WinCC, FactoryTalk, Ignition or equivalent • Networks: Ethernet/IP, Profinet, Modbus, DeviceNet • Robotics integration and VFD/servo systems • Basic scripting or programming (Python, C\+\+ preferred) • Machine vision systems • Familiarity with lean manufacturing and continuous improvement • Knowledge of safety systems — light curtains, safety relays, interlocks THE PERSON WE ARE LOOKING FOR Technical ability will get you through the door. What will make you stand out is how you work with people. This is a small company where knowledge lives with the team. You will need to build relationships quickly, communicate clearly under pressure and know how to get the best out of the people around you — not just the equipment in front of you. If that sounds like you, get in touch with Anthony at Pale Blue Dot® Recruitment for a confidential conversation. **WHAT IS ON OFFER** • Competitive salary based on experience • Permanent position with a stable, established MedTech manufacturer • Hands\-on role with real variety day to day • Small team environment where your work is seen and valued • Immediate interviews available for qualified candidates **Note:** By applying for this position, you may also be considered by Pale Blue Dot® Recruitment for other or future relevant vacancies. Pale Blue Dot® Recruitment — Experts in STEM Workforce Solutions

title: Azure Cloud Solution Architect
description: **Azure Cloud Solution Architect** **Salary:** Up to €95,000 **Contract:** Permanent, Full\-Time **Location:** Dublin **Onsite:** Hybrid **Role Overview:** Lead the design and delivery of scalable cloud solutions on Microsoft Azure, working closely with enterprise clients to define cloud strategies, modernise legacy systems, and implement secure, high\-performing architectures. **Key Responsibilities:** * Design end\-to\-end Azure\-based solution architectures (IaaS \& PaaS) * Translate business requirements into scalable, realisable system designs * Lead architecture decisions across Agile delivery teams * Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders to define solutions * Drive cloud migration strategies from legacy systems * Produce architecture documentation and present solutions to stakeholders * Contribute to IT strategy, governance, and technology decision\-making * Work across integrations, data platforms, web technologies, and enterprise systems **Required Skills:** * Minimum 3\+ years’ experience in a Solution Architect / Cloud Architect role * Strong expertise in Microsoft Azure (IaaS \& PaaS) * Azure certifications or willingness to achieve them * Background in software development (Java or .NET preferred) * Proven experience delivering complex, enterprise\-scale solutions * Strong stakeholder management and communication skills * Experience across integration, infrastructure, and application architecture * Ability to design multi\-system, enterprise\-level architectures * Strong problem\-solving and analytical skills **Nice to Have:** * Experience with AWS or Google Cloud * Exposure to project planning, budgeting, and delivery tracking * Experience supporting pre\-sales or bid processes * Third\-level degree in Computer Science or related field

title: Backend Engineer
description: Ingenio is partnering with a deep\-tech company operating at the intersection of **AI, molecular modelling, quantum physics, and high\-performance computing** . Backed by national and European funding, this award\-winning team is building the infrastructure powering next\-generation, AI\-driven drug development across pharma and biotech. They’re now hiring a **Backend Engineer** to help design and maintain the database and backend systems behind advanced scientific computation platforms. This is a hands\-on engineering position focused on **self\-hosted PostgreSQL infrastructure** , HPC workloads, and Python\-driven data pipelines. You’ll work closely with R\&D teams and senior technical leadership to ensure scalable, reliable backend systems supporting long\-running, parallelised scientific computation. **What You’ll Do:** * Design, build, and maintain a **self\-hosted PostgreSQL environment** * Configure and optimise databases for **parallel HPC workloads** * Own backup, recovery, disaster recovery, and data integrity * Define schemas, tables, permissions, and access controls * Contribute to the design and implementation of **ETL pipelines in Python** * Support observability and monitoring across database infrastructure **What You’ll Need:** * 3\+ years’ experience in database design, management, backup \& recovery * Strong PostgreSQL (or MySQL) experience, including **self\-hosting** * Solid Linux systems background * Experience with containers (Docker or Podman) * Strong Python fundamentals and clean software design principles * Experience with observability tools (e.g. Prometheus, Alembic, Barman or similar) * Clear communicator with an ownership mindset **Nice to Have:** * ETL orchestration tools (Prefect, Dagster, Airflow) * Parallel computing frameworks or schedulers (OpenMP, MPI, SLURM) Additional details: **Location:** South Dublin (Onsite 4 days per week) **Package:** Competitive salary \+ 10% bonus \+ Healthcare \+ Pension If you enjoy building robust backend systems that power cutting\-edge scientific research, this is a rare opportunity to work at the frontier of AI\-driven pharma innovation. 👉 Apply now or contact Ingenio for a confidential discussion.

title: Balance Management & Analysis
description: **Job Description:** **Job Title: Balance Management \& Analysis** **Corporate Title: Officer** **Location: Dublin** At Bank of America, we are guided by a common purpose to help make financial lives better through the power of every connection. We do this by driving Responsible Growth and delivering for our clients, teammates, communities and shareholders every day. Being a Great Place to Work and providing a culture of caring is core to how we drive Responsible Growth. We are intentional about fostering an inclusive workplace where every teammate has the opportunity to succeed, build a career and contribute to our shared success. This includes attracting and developing exceptional talent, recognizing and rewarding performance, and supporting our teammates’ physical, emotional, and financial wellness through affordable, competitive and flexible benefits. We value the unique perspectives individuals bring from all backgrounds and career paths \- whether shaped by military service, community college education, or a wide range of work and life experiences. These journeys foster resilience, leadership and innovation, strengthening our workforce and positively impact the communities we serve. Bank of America is committed to an in\-office culture that supports collaboration, engagement, and career development. Our approach includes clear in\-office expectations, while providing an appropriate level of flexibility based on role\-specific responsibilities and business needs. At Bank of America, you can build a successful career with opportunities to learn, grow, and make an impact. Join us! **Job Description:** This job is responsible for providing moderately complex research, reporting, and analytical support for the delivery of end\-to\-end support for Global Operations, sales, trading/underwriting business for securities, foreign exchange, fixed income, exchange traded derivatives, and over\-the\-counter derivatives across multiple Lines of Business. Key responsibilities include carrying out duties under general supervision and assisting teammates while following established procedures. Job expectations include performing Customer Identification Program and Customer Due Diligence refresh processes. **Responsibilities:** * Maintains accuracy of client portfolio using internal and external comparison tools * Performs research and conducts moderately complex analyses using established techniques, including transactions that require special handling and exception handling * Explores methods to enhance processes, further reduce risks, and boost client experience * Partners with colleagues on the trade and sales support teams, as well as external agents * Creates reports and liaises with internal partners to ensure proper client reporting and assist with regulatory reporting * Escalates potential risks and exposures to manager in a timely manner * Assists with ad\-hoc projects, new processes, or initiatives requiring coordination among subject matter experts representing multiple product areas or functions **Required Qualifications:** * Microsoft Excel experience **Desired Qualifications:** * Familiarity with the full Microsoft Office Suite, including Word, Outlook and Access * Experience with data analysis, including maintaining databases, collecting data, filtering and cleaning data, identifying patterns, interpreting data and presenting findings * Associate’s Degree or higher with a major in business, finance, accounting, economics or a related field * Experience in the financial services industry **Skills:** * Attention to Detail * Critical Thinking * Data Collection and Entry * Recording/Organizing Information * Research * Fraud Management * Interpret Relevant Laws, Rules, and Regulations * Problem Solving * Quality Assurance * Risk Management * Adaptability * Customer and Client Focus * Prioritization * Result Orientation * Written Communications

title: Barista
description: We're currently recruiting a driven **Barista** to help us create beautifully crafted drinks for Compass Group Ireland on a **full time basis** , contracted to **40** **hours per week, Monday to Friday (daytime).** As a **Barista** , you will use your skills to deliver first\-class service and perfectly crafted coffees to our customers. You'll get given every opportunity to progress within a company that invests in its people, celebrates individuality, and rewards and recognises employees who go beyond the plate. **Here's What You Need To Know Before Applying** **Your key responsibilities will include:** * Preparing perfectly blended drinks and serving high\-quality food that delights our customers * Keeping the bar area clean * Receiving and processing payments (cash and credit cards) * Being an enthusiastic team player and excellent communicator * Maintaining stock of clean mugs and plates * Learning about brewing methods, beverage blends, food preparation and presentation techniques * Check if brewing equipment operates properly and report any maintenance needs * Representing Compass Group Ireland and maintaining a positive brand image * Complying with Food Handling \& Hygiene standards * Complying with Health \& Safety regulations **Our Ideal Barista Will** * Be a brilliant communicator and easily build relationships * Have previous experience in customer service * Strive for excellence in an eager and motivated manner * Take initiative and make decisions that are right for our customers * Have Hands\-on experience with brewing equipment * Possess the ability to work under pressure * Demonstrate exceptional timekeeping and reliability At Compass Ireland, our people are our business. We are building a team who care about providing a great customer experience and want to grow and develop as we do. We are dedicated to investing in every colleague joining us and pride ourselves on being an equal opportunity employer; we celebrate diversity and are committed to building an inclusive environment for all our colleagues. title: Belgian Investment Counsellor
description: **Overview** Are you interested in working for an outstanding global investment firm and looking to take the next step in your career in Finance? Want to become an expert in and build a career advising clients on financial markets and investments? Want to focus on forging strong client relationships and providing exceptional client service? Fisher Investments Luxembourg – Dublin Branch is a European subsidiary of Fisher Investments, a privately\-held investment management firm that provides professional discretionary portfolio management services for high net worth private clients and some of the world's preeminent institutions. Our organisation has grown significantly over the past decade and we are looking for motivated candidates with finance backgrounds to join our team as Investment Counsellors to help us better the financial industry in Belgium as we continue our expansion in Europe. **The Opportunity:** The Investment Counsellor (IC) role is focused on managing relationships with high\-net\-worth clients in Belgium from our office in Dublin. Your ability to understand our clients' needs and educate them on the reasoning behind portfolio changes will be crucial to your success in this role. You will report to your Team Manager who will aid in your professional development. **The Day\-To\-Day:** * Build long standing relationships with our clients, ensuring they always receive exceptional service * Manage a book of high\-net\-worth relationships with approximately €100\-150 million in assets * Be the conduit between our clients, our Investment Policy Committee, our research and trading departments, and several service teams * Proactively update clients on portfolio changes, financial markets, our current market strategy and investment philosophy * Gain advanced finance and capital markets education through our training program **Your Qualifications:** * 2\+ years exposure to private clients within the banking /wealth management industry * A sound knowledge of financial concepts, markets, current events and the market * Native/Fluent French and English skills, Flemish \& Dutch is also highly beneficial * Connect with and educate a variety of audiences * Maintain a positive personality and empathise with clients in good times and bad * MiFID certification, training or the equivalent * CFA or similar education is beneficial **Your Benefits:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Luxembourg align with our clients’ priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It’s the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks’ top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to €50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. * Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS LUXEMBOURG IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: Building Services Engineer
description: **Building Services Engineer – North Dublin – €70k** Our client, a leading contractor, is currently delivering a data centre and commercial projects in North Dublin and is seeking a Building Services Engineer to join their team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to further develop their career within building services, with exposure to large\-scale residential developments and clear progression opportunities. **Package on offer:** Work from home Pension with employer contributions Full health insurance Annual salary review Generous annual leave **Responsibilities:** * Managing and monitoring the quality of M\&E installations from early coordination through to commissioning and handover * Supporting site teams with the coordination of mechanical and electrical services and assisting in resolving on\-site issues * Coordinating with design teams to ensure integration of M\&E systems with architectural and structural elements * Overseeing M\&E subcontractors to ensure delivery in line with programme and quality expectations * Providing guidance to junior team members to ensure consistency in M\&E standards across projects * Ensuring company procedures and best practices are implemented across all sites * Liaising with utility providers and local authorities to ensure timely provision of services including power, water, gas, and telecoms * Assisting in the development and updating of M\&E processes and documentation **Key skills required:** * Degree or equivalent qualification in Mechanical, Electrical, or Building Services Engineering * Minimum 3\+ years’ experience in a similar role, ideally with a developer or main contractor * Strong understanding of M\&E systems within residential construction projects * Interest in energy efficiency and modern building services technologies * Excellent communication and coordination skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Strong focus on deadlines and project delivery

title: Business Administration - Management Trainee Programme
description: **Summary** From our Head Office to the shop floor and everywhere in between our primary goal is to create and deliver a fantastic customer journey for everyone who shops at Lidl. With over 7,000 colleagues across our stores, warehouses and offices in Ireland and Northern Ireland we are a big and ambitious team. We know our people are our greatest asset and our success is down to the commitment and dedication of our team. Career development and training are of major importance to us. All employees are provided with the training and support they need to reach their full potential and to progress within the company. Structured, on\-the\-job training is the foundation of our training concept, giving our employees the opportunity for further development both professionally and personally. At Lidl we believe that training is the most important investment we can make in our people. The Business Administration – Management Trainee Programme offers exceptionally driven, bi\-lingual professionals and graduates a unique opportunity to complete a bespoke development programme that fits your qualifications, experience and career ambitions. Business Administration underpins our company’s success and with exposure to various essential business functions like Internal Audit, Legal \& Compliance, Management Accounting and Finance \& Accounting over an 18\-month period, this development programme will enable you to acquire the essential skills required to fast track your managerial career. **What You'll Do** **Internal Audit:** * Internal Audit ensure all areas of our business adhere to defined company processes \& procedures. * It assures that Lidl remain compliant and all potential business risks are protected against. * You will conduct independent and objective assessments of Operational, Administrative, Commercial, Central Service \& HR business functions. * Internal Audit will accelerate your operational and business procedural knowledge while also establishing cross functional working relationships with senior stakeholders. **Legal \& Compliance:** * The Legal \& Compliance department offer legal services and advice to the business. * They support contract services, legislation impact reviews, data protection, anti\-fraud / anti\-corruption and competition law oversight for the entire business. * L\&C also manage the investigation and defence of insurance incidents and claims. * Engaging with an expert panel of external medical consultants and engineers, L\&C are also responsible for mitigating business exposure to insurance costs. **Management Accounting:** * Management Accounting play a pivotal role within the business. * The controlling, analysis \& planning of all core business financials \& KPIs is the basis for informed strategic decision making. * Business Reporting \& Insights, Cost Management, Margin \& Profitability Analysis are some of the core responsibilities of Management Accounting. * Exposure to these functions during the programme supports your “big picture” mindset while also understanding how business performance is practically measured and controlled. **Finance \& Accounting:** * The Finance \& Accounting Department is responsible for processing and managing the finances of the company. * They control cash \& treasury management, bank reconciliations, timely payments to creditors and collections from debtors. * F\&A prepare financial statements on a monthly, quarterly and annual basis, in line with Lidl accounting procedures. **What You'll Need** * German language is essential. * Third\-level degree. * Proficiency with Google Workspace tools. * Excellent communication skills. * Motivation and drive. * Ability to work autonomously under pressure. * Full driving licence is required. **What You'll Receive** We offer a competitive and transparent salary system, that ensures pay equality across all positions at Lidl * €52,500 per annum * 20 days holidays per annum * Company car * International travel and training * Career mentorship from a company director * Excellent opportunities for career progression * Bespoke development programme based on current skills and career goals * Initial training and on\-going development from an experienced team member * Private employee medical insurance * A vast array of training opportunities from our in\-house training catalogue * Dynamic work environment * Modern office facility with free parking * On\-site gym and canteen * Hybrid working model \- up to 2 days remote working per week * Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network Lidl is an equal opportunities employer. If you have any particular requirements, we will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process. We value diversity and inclusion in our workplace and want to ensure that all applicants have the same opportunity regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, age, disability, race, religious belief, political opinion or membership of the traveller community.

title: Business Administration - Management Trainee Programme
description: **Summary** ----------- *From our Head Office to the shop floor and everywhere in between our primary goal is to create and deliver a fantastic customer journey for everyone who shops at Lidl.* With over 7,000 colleagues across our stores, warehouses and offices in Ireland and Northern Ireland we are a big and ambitious team. We know our people are our greatest asset and our success is down to the commitment and dedication of our team. Career development and training are of major importance to us. All employees are provided with the training and support they need to reach their full potential and to progress within the company. Structured, on\-the\-job training is the foundation of our training concept, giving our employees the opportunity for further development both professionally and personally. At Lidl we believe that training is the most important investment we can make in our people. The Business Administration – Management Trainee Programme offers exceptionally driven, bi\-lingual professionals and graduates a unique opportunity to complete a bespoke development programme that fits your qualifications, experience and career ambitions. Business Administration underpins our company’s success and with exposure to various essential business functions like Internal Audit, Legal \& Compliance, Management Accounting and Finance \& Accounting over an 18\-month period, this development programme will enable you to acquire the essential skills required to fast track your managerial career. **What you'll do** ------------------ **Internal Audit:** * Internal Audit ensure all areas of our business adhere to defined company processes \& procedures. * It assures that Lidl remain compliant and all potential business risks are protected against. * You will conduct independent and objective assessments of Operational, Administrative, Commercial, Central Service \& HR business functions. Internal Audit will accelerate your operational and business procedural knowledge while also establishing cross functional working relationships with senior stakeholders. * **Legal \& Compliance:** * The Legal \& Compliance department offer legal services and advice to the business. * They support contract services, legislation impact reviews, data protection, anti\-fraud / anti\-corruption and competition law oversight for the entire business. * L\&C also manage the investigation and defence of insurance incidents and claims. Engaging with an expert panel of external medical consultants and engineers, L\&C are also responsible for mitigating business exposure to insurance costs. * **Management Accounting:** * Management Accounting play a pivotal role within the business. * The controlling, analysis \& planning of all core business financials \& KPIs is the basis for informed strategic decision making. * Business Reporting \& Insights, Cost Management, Margin \& Profitability Analysis are some of the core responsibilities of Management Accounting. Exposure to these functions during the programme supports your “big picture” mindset while also understanding how business performance is practically measured and controlled. * **Finance \& Accounting:** * The Finance \& Accounting Department is responsible for processing and managing the finances of the company. * They control cash \& treasury management, bank reconciliations, timely payments to creditors and collections from debtors. F\&A prepare financial statements on a monthly, quarterly and annual basis, in line with Lidl accounting procedures. * **What you'll need** -------------------- * German language is essential. * Third\-level degree. * Proficiency with Google Workspace tools. * Excellent communication skills. * Motivation and drive. * Ability to work autonomously under pressure. * Full driving licence is required. **What you'll receive** ----------------------- We offer a competitive and transparent salary system, that ensures pay equality across all positions at Lidl * €52,500 per annum * 20 days holidays per annum * Company car * International travel and training * Career mentorship from a company director * Excellent opportunities for career progression * Bespoke development programme based on current skills and career goals * Initial training and on\-going development from an experienced team member * Private employee medical insurance * A vast array of training opportunities from our in\-house training catalogue * Dynamic work environment * Modern office facility with free parking * On\-site gym and canteen * Hybrid working model \- up to 2 days remote working per week Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network * Lidl is an equal opportunities employer. If you have any particular requirements, we will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process. We value diversity and inclusion in our workplace and want to ensure that all applicants have the same opportunity regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, age, disability, race, religious belief, political opinion or membership of the traveller community.

title: Business Analyst
description: Our Dublin based client are looking for an experienced and **Senior Business Analyst** to join their team on a daily rate contract Hybrid position based in Dublin with 2/3 days per week on site. Ideally looking for somebody who has experience in the Insurance sector **Job Responsibilities** * Gather, define and validate business requirements from multiple stakeholders for development and implementation of key products, tools and analytics * Lead and contribute towards project delivery and success * Analyse and understand business workflow and provide solutions for product enhancements and new initiatives and provide ongoing support for existing products * Collaborate with technology groups, business colleagues and other teams * Focus on the delivery of high quality, user\-centric products, and features * Work closely with software developers and other colleagues to ensure that they understand the requirements and the features * Support and co\-ordinate testing activities across all phases of projects including unit testing, integration testing, user acceptance testing and downstream testing * Collect and track feedback from the end users for future application and analytics design and provide recommendations in developing business solutions for business stakeholders **Experience Required** * Demonstrable experience in business analysis or similar role * Previous experience within a similar role with evidence of successful delivery of product and business change outcomes * Self\-starter with the ability to learn and work independently and as part of a team * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with knowledge of Agile processes and principles * Experience of taking ownership of stakeholder deliverables * Experience in digital product or platform development * Experience working with multi\-functional teams that include business partners, domain experts, analysts, UX designers, IT architects * Excellent communication skills with very strong interpersonal skills * Stakeholder Engagement Experience is a must * Experience with customer journe y design and customer experience implementations would be a bonus but not essential * Demonstrable ability to complete projects according to outlined scope, budget, and timeline **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities.

title: Business Development / New Sales Associate
description: Wage: €32,000 \- €240,000\+ p/a **The Role:** Generating new business via new HVAC Design and Build, PPM, and Shiva Air Sterilisation Products sales. The ideal candidate will be used to a commission\-based sales position, with excellent communications skills, able to work on their own initiative, and be self\-driven. As there is no salary cap, it is up to the performance of the candidate to determine how high their salary can potentially be. There is easy potential to earn €200k\+ per annum in a commission for the right individual, with the right experience, contacts, and drive. Self\-employed persons, or Ltd companies / distribution agency’s willing to dedicate members of staff to this task may also suit this role. **The Products:** The products are company IP owned, patented, and self\-manufactured ‘Air Sterilisation’ Products at the premium end of the market. Training will be provided to the candidate to operate and to learn the benefits of these niche products. The target markets which have been successful to date include, but are not limited to: Medical \& Healthcare, Office Settings, Retail \& Hospitality, Government \& Local Authorities, Schools \& Colleges. Previous experience with government contracts and/or dealing with the HSE (Ireland) is an advantage and is encouraged to be declared on application. There is a minimum quarterly sales target to meet, to ensure neither the company, nor the candidate is wasting each other’s time. Warm sales leads which naturally generate weekly via branding and advertisement campaigns will be provided to the candidate, however the ideal candidate will be expected to have their own methods of generating leads via existing contacts. This position is focused on the island of Ireland, (north and south). However, if a candidate has potential access for export sales markets\- it is welcome, but should be declared in the application as we currently have arrangements for distributors in markets including the UK, EU, US, and UAE. Job Type: Full\-time, Permanent Salary: €32,000\.00\-€240,000\.00\+ per year Benefits: * Guaranteed base retainer amount circa €600\+/week * Fixed Commission percentage on new sales * Flexible schedule * Work from home * Company Vehicle * Laptop, Tablet, Phone * Company Pension Scheme Expected start date: 19/04/2023  #### **Address** Shiva Air Conditioning Unit 8, Ballymount Cross Business Park, Ballymount Road Lower, Ballymount, Dublin 24, D24 PYK2  #### **Phone** (01\) 4539118  #### **Email** info@shiva.ie

title: Business Development Executive
description: Our client have a number of different businesses across the Irish real estate sector. They are seeking an entrepreneurial, ambitious and versatile candidate to work across three of their businesses. The first business is involved in construction, refurb and maintenance, the second business is in commercial property cleaning contractor and the third business that will require 50% of your time is an AI\-driven "Prop Tech" Property Management Software platform. The role will be based out of our clients offices in Rathmines but the vast majority of the time will out on the road meeting prospective customers and clients. If you're highly motivated with an entrepreneurial mindset and a keen interest in the property sector this is a fantastic opportunity to build and grow your career in a rapidly growing and ambitious company. **Responsibilities:** * Generate and convert new customers and business opportunities within key target markets. * Develop relationships with property management agencies, institutional landlords, developers, investors, and PRS industry partners. * Develop the ability to cross\-sell the services of all our businesses to both prospective and existing clients. * Identify and pursue strategic partnerships and referral opportunities. * Manage the full sales pipeline from lead generation through to onboarding. * Attend networking events, industry functions, and client meetings. **Experience \& Attributes:** * Experience in residential property lettings / management, PRS, estate agency services and / or PropTech. * Knowledge of residential real estate management, asset management, PRS and institutional landlord markets. * CRM and sales pipeline management experience. * Existing real estate industry relationships and referral network advantageous\- * An entrepreneurial and sales mindset that enjoys client relationship building.

title: Business Intelligence Developer
description: **Job Title: Data Analyst / Power BI Developer** **Location: Hybrid 2/3 days in Dublin city center location.** **Contract Duration: 6 Months (with potential to extend)** About the role: We are seeking a mid\-level Data Analyst / Power BI Developer to support the delivery and ongoing improvement of data products built on our Snowflake centred data platform and Power BI reporting stack. Reporting to the Data Product Manager and working closely with our Head of Data, Data Architect, engineers and business stakeholders, you will help test, validate and interpret data, develop effective reports and dashboards, and support both project deliveries and day\- to\-day BAU data issues. This role combines hands\-on data analysis, strong SQL and Power BI development with business / technical analysis skills. You will be comfortable working through detailed requirements, performing system and user acceptance testing, and helping ensure that data products are accurate, usable and aligned with business needs. Key Responsibilities: * Work with the Data Product Manager to understand business requirements and translate them into clear, testable data and reporting specifications. * Analyse, profile and validate data directly in Snowflake using SQL, ensuring data quality, consistency and completeness across key datasets. * Design and build Power BI reports and dashboards that are intuitive, performant and aligned to agreed data models and definitions. * Support the development and enhancement of semantic models, datasets and calculations in Power BI to enable self\-service analytics and standardised reporting. * Execute system testing and support UAT for data warehouse and reporting changes, including test case design, test execution and defect triage. * Investigate and resolve BAU data and reporting issues, working with Snowflake engineers, Power BI developers and IT support where required. * Contribute to data quality monitoring by helping define and validate checks, reconciliations and controls across key data products. * Document report specifications, data mappings, test evidence and user guides to support effective knowledge transfer and BAU support. * Provide day\-to\-day support to business users on Power BI reports (queries, minor enhancements, issue investigation), helping to drive adoption and a data\-driven culture. * Collaborate with the Data Architect and engineers to ensure solutions are technically robust, secure and consistent with enterprise data architecture and standards Ideal Candidate Profile: * 3–5 years’ experience in a Data Analyst, BI Developer, Technical Business Analyst or similar role, ideally within financial services or another data\-rich, regulated environment. * Strong SQL skills with proven experience querying and validating data in a cloud data warehouse environment (e.g. Snowflake). * Solid hands\-on experience with Power BI Desktop and Power BI Service, including building datasets, reports and dashboards for business users. * Experience contributing to or maintaining semantic data models and DAX calculations, with an understanding of how model design impacts performance. * Demonstrated experience supporting both project work (requirements, system testing, UAT support) and BAU data / reporting issue resolution. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to interpret complex data and communicate insights in clear, non\-technical language. * Experience working with a range of stakeholders (business teams, Data Product Manager, Data Architect, engineers, developers) and balancing competing priorities. * Good understanding of data governance, data quality and controls, with an appreciation of operating in a regulated environment. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with attention to detail and a collaborative, delivery\-focused approach. * Third\-level qualification in Business, Finance, Technology, Data or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience. Personal Attributes: * Curious and proactive, with a strong interest in data, analytics and continuous improvement. * Someone with a quality mindset that shows attention to detail * Passionate about solving data puzzles * Comfortable working in a small, hands\-on data team and adapting to evolving priorities and ways of working. * Customer\-focused mindset, keen to understand business problems and translate them into practical data and reporting solutions.

title: Business Process & Automation Intern
description: This role has been designed as ‘Hybrid’ with an expectation that you will work on average 2 days per week from an HPE office. **Who We Are** Hewlett Packard Enterprise is the global edge\-to\-cloud company advancing the way people live and work. We help companies connect, protect, analyze, and act on their data and applications wherever they live, from edge to cloud, so they can turn insights into outcomes at the speed required to thrive in today’s complex world. Our culture thrives on finding new and better ways to accelerate what’s next. We know varied backgrounds are valued and succeed here. We have the flexibility to manage our work and personal needs. We make bold moves, together, and are a force for good. If you are looking to stretch and grow your career our culture will embrace you. Open up opportunities with HPE. **Job Description** **Hewlett Packard Enterprise Financial Services** (HPEFS) offers university students the opportunity to join a leading process transformation team through our Internship Program. You’ll be immersed in a high\-impact, collaborative environment that bridges strategic process design, intelligent automation, and business value realization. As part of our **Process \& Operations Transformation (POT)** organization, you’ll be exposed to diverse initiatives that span process mapping and engineering, automation, internal stakeholder support, and strategic problem\-solving and have the chance to focus on a particular area to make an impact. You’ll work alongside process owners, automation analysts, and transformation leads who are driving change across our business. This is a dynamic opportunity for students who want to build practical experience in business process management, data\-driven decision\-making, and digital execution. **Start Date: 04\. July 2026 or later** **Location: Dublin Ireland (Hybrid)** **Mandatory: You must be enrolled at a university throughout the duration of the internship** **What You’ll Do** As a member of the POT internship program, you may: * Support process engineering initiatives by helping build process maps, identifying inefficiencies, and observing real\-world workflow challenges. * Assist process owners and continuous improvement leads with documentation, stakeholder engagement, and change management tasks. * Participate in automation and analytics projects, where you may help gather requirements for RPA, explore business use cases for AI tools, or support dashboard/report design. * Contribute to value realization and initiative tracking, helping measure project outcomes and business impact. * Be part of ad hoc focused support teams for urgent transactional bottlenecks, problem analysis, or team deep dives into operational pain points. * Collaborate with transformation leads to prepare materials, stakeholder summaries, or project plans related to cross\-functional priorities. * Gain exposure to industry tools and techniques like Lean Six Sigma, process mining, and agile work management. **What You’ll Need** * Actively pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Industrial Engineering, Operations Management, Data Analytics, or a related discipline. * Strong analytical, communication, and problem\-solving skills. * Enthusiasm for business process improvement, digital tools, and real\-world operational challenges. * Ability to manage your time, balance competing priorities, and thrive in a dynamic work environment. * Curiosity, a learning mindset, and interest in working with a diverse, globally distributed team. **What You’ll Gain** * Hands\-on experience contributing to enterprise process transformation projects. * Exposure to real business challenges and the opportunity to help shape how work gets done. * 1:1 mentorship and the ability to shadow process, automation, and transformation experts. * A strong foundation in how modern companies use technology and data to simplify and improve operations. **About Our Team** The **Process \& Operations Transformation (POT)** team plays a central role in how HPEFS drives simplification, standardization, and intelligent automation across our global operations. Interns become part of a community focused on continuous improvement, with clear exposure to both business strategy and digital execution. **At HPE, We’re** * Human Rights Campaign’s 2023\-2024 Corporate Equality Index * Great Place to Work’s Best Workplace for Parents 2023 * Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For® 2024 * Ethisphere World’s Most Ethical Company 2024 * The Civic 50 Honoree * JUST Jobs Leader 2024 * Forbes 2024 America’s Best Employers For Diversity * America's Most Just Companies 2024 * Fortune Best Workplaces in Texas™ 2024 * Bay Area's Best Places to Work 2024 **Ready to take the next step?** Open up opportunities with HPE. **Additional Skills** **What We Can Offer You:** **Health \& Wellbeing** We strive to provide our team members and their loved ones with a comprehensive suite of benefits that supports their physical, financial and emotional wellbeing. **Personal \& Professional Development** We also invest in your career because the better you are, the better we all are. We have specific programs catered to helping you reach any career goals you have — whether you want to become a knowledge expert in your field or apply your skills to another division. **Unconditional Inclusion** We are unconditionally inclusive in the way we work and celebrate individual uniqueness. We know varied backgrounds are valued and succeed here. We have the flexibility to manage our work and personal needs. We make bold moves, together, and are a force for good. **Let's Stay Connected** Follow @HPECareers on Instagram to see the latest on people, culture and tech at HPE. \#ireland \#hpefs, \#interns **Job** Administration **Job Level** N/A HPE is an Equal Employment Opportunity/ Veterans/Disabled/LGBT employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, or any other protected category, and all decisions we make are made on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need. Our goal is to be one global team that is representative of our customers, in an inclusive environment where we can continue to innovate and grow together. Please click here: Equal Employment Opportunity. **Hewlett Packard Enterprise is EEO Protected Veteran/ Individual with Disabilities.** HPE will comply with all applicable laws related to employer use of arrest and conviction records, including laws requiring employers to consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories. **Recruitment Fraud Alert** We have become aware of an increase in fraudulent recruitment activities in which individuals impersonate our company or authorized recruitment agencies to offer fake employment opportunities. These scams may occur through false websites, emails, social media, or chat\-based applications and often aim to obtain personal information or money. Please note that Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), its direct and indirect subsidiaries and affiliated companies, and its authorized recruitment agencies/vendors will never charge a candidate a registration fee, hiring fee, or any other fee in connection with its recruitment and hiring process. We also never request personal information such as back account details, Social Security numbers, or national IDs via social media or chat applications. All legitimate job opportunities will come through official company channels, and candidates are responsible for verifying the credentials of any third party claiming to represent the company. Any reliance on fraudulent communication is at the individual’s own risk, and HPE disclaims legal liability for any resulting damages. If you suspect recruitment fraud, do not share personal information or make any payments and report the incident to your local authorities immediately.

title: Business Systems Analysis (BSA) Associate
description: **Before you apply to a job, select your language preference from the options available at the top right of this page.** Explore your next opportunity at a Fortune Global 500 organization. Envision innovative possibilities, experience our rewarding culture, and work with talented teams that help you become better every day. We know what it takes to lead UPS into tomorrow—people with a unique combination of skill \+ passion. If you have the qualities and drive to lead yourself or teams, there are roles ready to cultivate your skills and take you to the next level. **Job Description:** The Business Systems Analysis (BSA) Associate is a member of the Strategic Decisions Support (SDS) team, and supports Global Account Center (GAC) business analysis processes under guidance, contributing to the delivery of technology\-driven solutions aligned to business needs. The role involves assisting in problem identification, requirements analysis, and solution recommendation within defined methodologies and governance frameworks. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the elicitation, analysis, and documentation of business and system requirements across accounting based projects of varying complexity * Assist SDS team members in identifying business problems, evaluating solution options, and recommending improvements * Track and document progress of assigned tasks, ensuring alignment with project objectives and adherence to quality standards * Analyze accounting\-related project requests and translate them into clear, structured system requirements * Evaluate and decompose existing accounting processes to identify opportunities for efficiency, automation, and optimization * Apply **GAAP principles** and accounting data analysis techniques to recommend compliant improvements to business practices * Contribute to program governance activities, including timeline tracking, risk identification, and stakeholder reporting * Identify and escalate risks proactively, particularly those impacting project scope, timelines, or deliverables * Support resolution of project performance issues in line with organizational policies and procedures * Maintain and manage project plans to ensure timely completion of milestones and tasks * Collaborate with cross\-functional and global teams in a hybrid working environment * Coordinate and facilitate both in\-person and virtual meetings to drive project progress and stakeholder alignment **Qualified candidates will have the following Key Skills:** * Excellent communication, organizational, and influencing skills * High levels of energy and commitment to hitting deadlines * Must be an excellent team player and provide leadership to project stakeholders * Will actively seek out efficiencies and work with stakeholders to formulate and drive solutions * Self\-starter, capable of using initiative * Strong capacity to communicate actions, implications of data and drive action * Ability to embrace and contribute to culture of process improvement * Robust evaluation and critical thinking skills * Qualified or partly qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) \& bachelor’s degree * Experience with data analysis and project management tools – Preferred **Employee Type:** Permanent UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation.

title: C# Full-Stack Developer - Experienced Hire
description: **Overview** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. At Susquehanna International Group, our technologists play a critical role in shaping the future of electronic trading. We build high\-performance systems designed to compete in some of the most demanding and latency\-sensitive markets in the world. Our developers collaborate closely with quantitative researchers and infrastructure teams to solve complex technical and strategic problems that drive significant business impact. We are looking for a full\-stack developer with strong proficiency in C\#/.NET to join a fast\-paced, iterative development team delivering business\-critical systems. This role suits a pragmatic technologist who understands end\-to\-end system impact, communicates clearly, and delivers business value quickly without compromising long\-term stability. Successful candidates balance technical excellence with practical decision\-making, take ownership of their work, and collaborate closely with both technical and business stakeholders. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, build, and maintain robust full\-stack applications using C\#/.NET and related technologies * Operate in an iterative development environment using lightweight specifications and fast feedback loops * Collaborate closely with traders, business users, support teams, and fellow developers * Contribute to system and application architecture with a focus on scalability, resilience, and performance * Take ownership of systems across the full lifecycle, from requirements and design through to production support * Support production environments at 2nd level, ensuring system stability and prioritising operational improvements * Participate actively in design discussions, reviews, and knowledge\-sharing sessions * Apply pragmatic decision\-making guided by speed, capacity, stability, and risk **What We're Looking For** **Skills and Experience** * Proficiency in C\#/.NET and WPF * Experience in at least one additional programming language (e.g. Python or JavaScript) * Solid understanding of software design, debugging, and problem solving * Experience working with distributed systems and business\-critical applications * Familiarity with modern development tools and practices (e.g. Git\-based workflows, CI/CD) * Experience working across the full software development lifecycle, including production support * Strong written and verbal communication skills, particularly in collaborative, fast\-moving environments **Personal Attributes** * Pragmatic and delivery\-focused, without sacrificing code quality or long\-term maintainability * Strong sense of ownership and accountability * Clear, concise communicator with good judgment on when and how to engage others * Curious, adaptable, and motivated to understand both technical systems and business context * Collaborative, professional, and resilient under pressure **Nice to Have** * Experience in financial, trading, or time\-sensitive environments * Exposure to cloud platforms, containerisation, or system monitoring * Experience with databases and writing complex queries * Experience in software performance tuning for high throughput or low latency * Experience contributing to documentation, mentoring, or engineering standards Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. title: C++ Developer - Experienced Hire
description: **Overview** At Susquehanna International Group, our technologists play a critical role in shaping the future of electronic trading. We build high\-performance systems designed to compete in some of the most demanding and latency\-sensitive markets in the world. Our developers collaborate closely with quantitative researchers and infrastructure teams to solve complex technical and strategic problems that drive significant business impact. We're looking for a C\+\+ Strategy Developer to join our Trading Technology team in Dublin. In this role, you’ll design, build, and optimise high\-frequency trading systems that process massive data streams in real time. You’ll work side\-by\-side with experienced technologists and quantitative analysts, gaining deep exposure to the logic and technology that powers automated decision\-making in global financial markets. Trading experience is beneficial but not required. **What You’ll Do** * Design and develop low\-latency, high\-throughput trading applications using modern C\+\+ in a real\-time environment. * Collaborate with quantitative researchers and technologists to implement and refine strategies. * Build and tune systems to achieve optimal performance, reliability, and scalability in a competitive trading landscape. * Optimise and troubleshoot infrastructure for latency, throughput, and system bottlenecks. * Own the full software development lifecycle—from idea to production—while contributing to our long\-term architecture and tooling. **What We're Looking For** * A degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field. Professional experience developing performance\-critical applications in modern C\+\+ (C\+\+11/14/17/20\). * Strong command of multi\-threading, memory management, IPC, STL, Boost, and network/socket programming. * Deep understanding of performance tuning, profiling, and optimisation methodologies. * Familiarity with designing high\-performance systems in real\-time environments (prior trading or financial industry experience is a plus, but not required). * Passion for solving challenging technical problems, iterating quickly, and writing clean, maintainable code. **Why You'll Love It Here** * Real Impact: Your work will be central to Susquehanna's technology operations, directly impacting decisions and performance in global markets. * Collaboration: You'll partner directly with quants and technologists, working on cross\-functional teams where your input truly matters. * Challenge \& Growth: We value individuals who thrive on solving hard problems and continuously push to learn and improve. * Culture of Excellence: Our flat structure and informal environment encourage innovation, fast feedback, and hands\-on ownership. * Benefits \& Perks: Susquehanna offers a highly competitive compensation package, along with extensive benefits designed to support you both professionally and personally. **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. **Equal Opportunity Statement** We’re committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. title: Civil Engineer Water
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Dedicated to sustainable development, Arup is a collective of designers, consultants and experts working globally. Founded to be humane and excellent, we collaborate with our clients and partners using imagination, technology, and rigour to shape a better world. **The Opportunity** Due to significant growth in our Water Business in Ireland and internationally, we are currently recruiting for a junior civil engineer to join our water team. The position available is for a civil/structural engineer with 2 to 4 years’ experience and a background in the delivery of infrastructure projects. Ideally this experience would have been gained in the design/delivery of flood relief schemes or water infrastructure projects, but all relevant civil engineering experience will be considered. Candidates will be expected to have a sound understanding and experience of the multi\-disciplinary nature of infrastructure projects. The ideal candidates will be looking to further their career and gain invaluable experience within an established multi\-disciplinary engineering consultancy in a position of responsibility. This is a permanent position and offers the successful candidate long\-term opportunities. **As a Civil Engineer At Arup, You Will** * Prepare technical designs for projects in accordance with approved design standards and procedures in line with the delivery programme. * Ensure that relevant legislation, regulations, policies, strategies, and technical guidance is interpreted, followed and communicated across the team with guidance from senior members of staff. * Implement the appropriate QA procedures for all deliverables and ensure compliance with H\&S requirements; and * Communicate effectively and in a team environment with a broad cross\-section of individuals. This would typically include but is not limited to; water team colleagues and project managers, other internal design disciplines, clients and contractors. **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for someone passionate about sustainable design. These are essential skills and qualifications for the position: * A minimum of a bachelor’s degree in a Civil Engineering or Structural Engineering discipline is required for this role (equivalent to Level 8 in the Irish NFQ). * A Master’s degree would be beneficial but not essential. * Experience in the design of flood relief schemes, pumping stations and drainage pipelines will be an advantage. * Ability to undertake structural calculations to support the design of flood defence elements using first principles and relevant structural modelling software. * Prepare and review design drawings using AutoCAD, Civil3D or GIS software in conjunction with project technicians. * An ability to prepare technical reports, correspondence, and other outputs in accordance with approved standards and procedures in line with the delivery programme. * Passionate about driving digital delivery on projects and keeping appraised of latest industry developments around digital and BIM. * Understanding of wider engineering project requirements e.g. geotechnics, utilities, risk management etc and ability to tailor civil engineering interventions to suit wider project aspirations. * Undertake site inspections during construction to ensure contractor compliance with the design requirements. * Knowledge of hydraulic and hydrological principles and relevant industry software. * Be aware of the Irish regulatory and planning framework, together with experience in the design and delivery of civil engineering projects through the planning process. * Communicate effectively and in a team environment with a broad cross\-section of individuals. This would typically include, but is not limited to; flooding team colleagues and project managers, other internal design disciplines, clients and contractors. **We Also Value** * Enthusiasm for a career in the water sector, underpinned through knowledge and understanding of our sector and work. * An organised approach and the ability to plan\-ahead to ensure deadlines are met. * A creative approach to problem solving and seek to improve existing ways of working. * The aptitude to work on your own initiative and be proactive. * A strong sense of appropriate ethical practice. A professional, trustworthy and approachable attitude. **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

description: Location Dublin, Dublin, Ireland Capabilities Civil and Structural Office Setup Office/Onsite Job ID \#40043 Market Enterprise Functions At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission\-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting\-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good. generations. * Assist the NEC4 Project Supervisor in overseeing all construction assurance activities on site * Inspect and monitor workmanship, quality and safety across civil works including earthworks, drainage, bridges and structures * Ensure works comply with Works Requirements, specifications, Irish Building Regulations and Health \& Safety standards * Support the establishment and within the team * Act as a key point of contact between site, project core teams and relevant stakeholders * Encourage knowledge transfer and collaboration across consultant and client teams * Support reporting requirements in line with governance and PMO expectations * Embed a culture of continuous improvement and best practice across site activities Here's what you'll need * 5\+ years’ experience in construction management / supervision on large‑scale road or infrastructure projects * Strong knowledge of civil and structural works and the ability to interpret drawings and specifications * Experience working within Irish Building Regulations, planning processes and Health \& Safety requirements * A proactive, solutions‑focused approach with excellent attention to detail * Strong communication skills and the ability to build effective working relationships * The confidence to manage multiple priorities and contribute to team leadership *Qualifications:* * Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering (or equivalent) * Safepass accreditation * Chartered / Incorporated / Associate Engineer status or working towards same (desirable) ***Why Join Jacobs \| What We Offer*** -------------------------------------- At Jacobs, you’ll find more than a job — you’ll find a career with purpose. ### **Meaningful Work** Be part of a landmark infrastructure project that will shape
