title: Network Engineer
description: Our client, based in Dublin, is seeking an experienced Senior Network Engineer to support and enhance enterprise network infrastructure within a large\-scale operational environment. This is a hands\-on contract role focused on firewall administration, network security operations, and infrastructure support across on\-premise and cloud environments. The successful candidate will play a key role in maintaining secure, resilient, and high\-performing network services. **Key Responsibilities** * Administration and support of enterprise firewall platforms including technologies such as Fortinet, Check Point, Palo Alto, or Azure Firewall * Management of firewall policies, NAT, VPN connectivity (site\-to\-site and remote access), and security rulebases * Troubleshooting complex network, routing, and connectivity issues across LAN, WAN, and cloud environments * Support and maintenance of core network services including switching, routing, DNS, DHCP, and network segmentation * Implementation and support of network security controls in collaboration with cybersecurity teams * Participation in infrastructure upgrades, network changes, and implementation projects * Operational support for cloud networking environments, preferably Microsoft Azure * Maintenance of network documentation, configurations, and operational procedures * Knowledge transfer and collaboration with internal technical teams **Required Skills \& Experience** * 5–10\+ years’ experience in Network Engineering or Infrastructure Engineering roles * Strong hands\-on experience managing enterprise firewall environments * Excellent troubleshooting and diagnostic capability across complex networks * Experience supporting enterprise LAN/WAN infrastructure * Strong understanding of networking protocols and security principles * Ability to work independently in a fast\-paced operational environment **Desirable Experience** * Experience with Azure networking and cloud security services * Exposure to regulated or compliance\-driven environments * Familiarity with security frameworks and governance standards * Experience working within large enterprise IT operations teams **How to Apply** To apply, please send your latest CV outlining your relevant experience.

title: Operations & Brand Intern, Summer @ Mafia Matcha
description: ***First filter: you already drink matcha. Like, properly. You have a tin at home, you have opinions about it, and you’ve definitely paid €6 for one in town and not regretted it. If that’s not you, this one isn’t for you!*** We’re Mafia Matcha \- an Irish matcha brand now stocked in over 200 coffee shops and retailers across Ireland, plus our own DTC channel and pop\-ups around Dublin. We’re a small team building something a bit louder, a bit more fun, and a lot better\- tasting than what’s already out there \- and we’re growing fast enough that the founder genuinely needs a second pair of hands across ops and brand this summer. This isn’t a “shadow the team and write a report at the end” internship. You’ll be in the warehouse packing real orders, in the office throwing ideas around, and out at pop\-ups talking matcha with strangers \- often on the same day. **What a week actually looks like:** One day you’re at our Sandyford warehouse packing 30 orders, restocking, and making sure the shelves don’t descend into chaos. The next you’re at the office helping us figure out which 15 creators to send PR boxes to, drafting the outreach, and getting the boxes out the door. Saturday you might be at a pop\-up in town whisking matcha for people who’ve never tried it and convincing them they’ve been missing out. Monday you bring back three ideas from what you saw at the pop\-up and we actually try one of them. That’s the job. Ops \+ brand \+ customer\-facing, mixed together, every week different. **The split, roughly** * \~50% Operations \- packing orders, warehouse organisation, stock, the day\-to\-day of a small brand running properly * \~30% Brand \& PR \- running our PR/UGC outreach end\-to\-end: building lists, writing outreach, prepping boxes, following up, learning what works * \~20% Pop\-ups \& events \- repping the brand in person, serving matcha, educating people, being the face of Mafia Matcha out in the wild The split flexes week to week. Some weeks are heavier on events, some on ops. Some weekend availability is needed \- pop\-ups happen when our customers are free, which is often Saturdays. You’ll thrive here if you: * Are a 2nd or 3rd year Business\-oriented student (marketing, commerce, entrepreneurship, management \- anything in that family) * Genuinely drink matcha and can talk about it without faking it * Are obsessed with brands and social \- you follow visual brands, you screenshot good packaging, you have opinions about why some DTC brands fly and others die * Bring ideas without being asked. If you notice the order labels could be laid out better, you say so. If a creator we should DM isn\&\#39;t on our list, you add them. * Are organised enough that nothing slips, and chilled enough to roll with a startup week where the plan changes on Wednesday * Are warm and confident with strangers at pop\-ups \- not performative, just genuinely happy to chat about something you love * Have your own car \- non\-negotiable. You\&\#39;ll be moving between Sandyford and pop\-ups around Dublin with equipment. * Live in South Dublin \- Dundrum, Stillorgan, Sandyford, Foxrock, Blackrock, Dún Laoghaire etc. Max \~20 min by car from Sandyford. **This isn’t the job for you if:** * You don’t drink matcha and you’re hoping you’ll “grow into it” * You don’t drive or don’t have access to a car * You want a structured corporate internship with a defined programme and a manager telling you what to do every hour * The idea of packing orders or doing manual warehouse work feels beneath you (we all do it, including the founders) * You’re not based in or near South Dublin and the commute would be a stretch * “Just doing what’s on your job description” is your default setting **Logistics:** * Pay: €16,5/hour \- paid for every hour worked, including weeks that run longer * Hours: 20/week base, some weeks more with prior agreement * Start: Monday 22nd June 2026 * End: Sunday 14th September 2026, with strong possibility of extension into a part\-time role through the academic year (hours flexed around your timetable). That’s the actual goal here, we’d rather invest in one person who sticks for another year or two than re\-hire every summer. * Location: Sandyford\-based, with pop\-ups around Dublin and beyond * Application deadline: 9th June 2026 **How to apply:** Send the following to jobs@mafiamatcha.com: 1\. Your CV (one page, please) 2\. A short intro \- written, voice note, or Loom, whatever you prefer. Tell us why Mafia Matcha specifically, and your honest matcha story (when, how often, favourite way to drink it). 3\. Pick one: a) Name 3 creators or press contacts you’d want us to send a PR box to, and one line on why each b) Tell us one thing about Mafia Matcha’s social / brand / website you’d change, and what you’d try instead Follow us before you apply: @mafiamatcha IG/TikTok \- it’ll tell you more about who we are than any job ad can:)

title: Operations Scheduler | Estimator
description: **Company Description** CPC Ltd Electrical Contracting Services, based in Wicklow, specializes in delivering high\-quality electrical solutions for commercial, industrial, and residential clients. The company is built on international project experience, including work in Australia, emphasizing safety, compliance, and professional execution. CPC Ltd takes a selective approach to projects, ensuring precision, performance, and long\-term reliability. With a strong reputation for excellence, the company is committed to meeting the highest standards in the electrical contracting industry. Operations Scheduler / Estimator Electrical Contracting – Wicklow Town We are seeking an organised and motivated **Operations Scheduler / Estimator** to join our growing electrical contracting business based in Wicklow Town. This is a key role within the company involving the coordination of electricians, scheduling of projects and service works, preparation of quotations and estimates, customer communication, and day\-to\-day operational support. The successful candidate will work closely with management, site teams, suppliers, and customers to ensure projects and service works are delivered efficiently and professionally. Key Responsibilities * Scheduling electricians and coordinating daily works * Managing reactive maintenance and service callouts * Preparing quotations and estimates for electrical works * Liaising with customers, suppliers, and subcontractors * Ordering materials and coordinating deliveries * Tracking project progress and updating schedules * Supporting project and office operations * Assisting with job costing, administration, and reporting Requirements * Previous experience in scheduling, estimating, operations, or administration within construction, electrical, or trades environments * Strong organisational and communication skills * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment * Good IT skills including Microsoft Office * Experience using scheduling or job management systems preferred * Electrical or construction industry experience advantageous What We Offer * Competitive salary based on experience * Long\-term opportunity within a growing business * Supportive working environment * Career progression opportunities * Company phone and laptop provided This role would suit someone with experience in electrical contracting administration, construction coordination, service scheduling, estimating, or project support who is looking to grow within a busy and expanding company. To apply, please send your CV and cover letter to info@cpcltd.ie

title: Optical Assistant
description: Are you a caring individual looking to start your career as an Optical Assistant? You’ve come to the right place. We aren’t looking for people with years of experience and heaps of qualifications, just passionate, hard\-workers ready to confidently welcome and help customers as they walk through our doors. And once you’ve found your feet as a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t just have to stop there. We have plenty of professional training and development in other business areas, as well as the support of experienced Opticians and Technicians supporting you on your journey. So whatever you want your career to look like, you can bring it to life at Specsavers. **Our store** Based in Navan, our store has great career progression opportunities! **What’s on offer?** As well as all the support you need and great training and development opportunities, we have a whole load of benefits on offer for you to enjoy. These include: * Full time \- 40 hours, weekend working is essential * €14\.15 an hour with a salary review after probation * Great career progression opportunities in a friendly store **What we’re looking for?** Some of the responsibilities of this role include: * Welcoming customers into store * Booking in eye tests * Pre\-screening patients * Providing style advice * Measuring frames * Taking phone call **Find out more** If you are comfortable with the responsibilities we’re looking for and are excited by this opportunity, we’re excited to hear from you. Got what we’re looking for? This job will close when it's filled, so don't delay, apply today!

title: Optical NDE II, Optical Long Haul Network Deployment
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Do you like to use networking concepts and system development engineering to deliver simple, sustainable and repeatable solutions? Would you like your work to play a key role in driving the stability and sustainability of next\-generation IP networks? Amazon Web Services is looking for an Optical Network Development Engineer to support and deploy one of the world’s largest and most complex telecommunication networks. Our goal is to become “The Infrastructure Platform” to the world and customers demand the highest quality and reliability for their services. As we expand at a tremendous rate it is our responsibility to maintain that quality and reliability. We look for innovative ways to automate and scale our network as we expand, while driving complex issues to resolution. AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. Key job responsibilities As an Optical Deployment Engineer working within AWS Infrastructure you will: * Design of optical solutions, hands\-on to support deployment and manage rollout of AWS global DWDM network; * Drive optical scaling tasks including fiber quality reviews and generate cutsheets and configs to meet the demand of AWS customers; * Define and configure management solution to support optical network remote monitoring and device visibility; * Work closely with other AWS Networking teams (field tech and fiber teams) to ensure optical network is scaled in time to support business; * Review and implement changes on optical systems using manual CLI config or automated process via script; * Ensure that optical network deployment tasks adhere to the defined AWS network standards; * Execute implementation tasks following operational excellence standards and propose improvements in AWS DWDM network quality and reliability; * Design and implement scalable operational processes to sustain and expand our optical network infrastructure; * Lead end\-to\-end product evaluation and drive strategic commercial tender processes, ensuring optimal vendor selection and cost efficiency * Troubleshoot complex problems on optical networks and eventually propose innovative solutions to improve the deployment agility; * Lead and execute operational excellence programs that measurably improve DWDM network quality and reliability, reducing service\-impacting events and raising the bar on optical network performance; Our ideal candidate is highly autonomous, possesses strong written and verbal communication skills, strong project management and time management skills and has a successful track record of troubleshooting complex network problems and delivering innovative solutions. The desire and ability to work in a fast paced, collaborative environment is essential. This is an amazing opportunity in terms of responsibility, interesting challenges and high visibility. A day in the life This role oversees deployment of Metro and Long Haul systems and all processes related to those deliverables. It includes the design of the projects, generation of all technical documents, updating internal tools, deployment of systems in the real network and handover for operations. Also the deployment team is responsible to participate in testing new solutions and define technical requirements. Automation is a very important topic in this team to improve the deployment process, reduce the manual activities and make interaction between internal tools. About the team Internet Edge provides high\-performing Internet connectivity for a wide range of use cases, such as retail (amazon.com), video streaming (Prime Video), content delivery (CloudFront), DNS (Route53\), AWS services (such as EC2/S3\), consumer devices (such as FireTV/Kindle/Echo), and more. Within AWS Networking, the Internet Edge organization is responsible for connecting Amazon to the global Internet. With connections to thousands of unique external networks in hundreds of locations across the world, our mission is to deliver an Internet connectivity experience that sets the global standard for performance, availability, security, and cost, making Amazon the clear choice for our customers’ needs. The Long Haul Deployment team is responsible for the installation and commissioning of the DWDM systems for Optical Backbone. Our mission is to deliver unconstrained inter\-regional transport at the lowest cost, with the most available product conceivable to AWS customers, making it invisible. The team vends capacity to internet backbone customers that provides low latency, high availability and supports features our customers expect. About AWS Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Information Technology, a related technical field or equivalent experience * 4\+ years' experience with major DWDM platforms; * 4\+ years' experience with optical planning, modelling tools and hands\-on implementation; * Experience with the rollout of large DWDM networks in Metro and Long Haul spaces; **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Knowledge of networking fundamentals * Knowledge of network design, protocols and Layer 1/2 troubleshooting * Experience with optical planning and modelling tools; and the rollout of large DWDM networks. * Optical network design, planning and implementation skills; Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. description: **Description** Do you like to use networking concepts and system development engineering to deliver simple, sustainable and repeatable solutions? Would you like your work to play a key role in driving the stability and sustainability of next\-generation IP networks? Amazon Web Services is looking for an Optical Network Development Engineer to support and deploy one of the world’s largest and most complex telecommunication networks. Our goal is to become “The Infrastructure Platform” to the world and customers demand the highest quality and reliability for their services. As we expand at a tremendous rate it is our responsibility to maintain that quality and reliability. We look for innovative ways to automate and scale our network as we expand, while driving complex issues to resolution. AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. Key job responsibilities As an Optical Deployment Engineer working within AWS Infrastructure you will: * Design of optical solutions, hands\-on to support deployment and manage rollout of AWS global DWDM network; * Drive optical scaling tasks including fiber quality reviews and generate cutsheets and configs to meet the demand of AWS customers; * Define and configure management solution to support optical network remote monitoring and device visibility; * Work closely with other AWS Networking teams (field tech and fiber teams) to ensure optical network is scaled in time to support business; * Review and implement changes on optical systems using manual CLI config or automated process via script; * Ensure that optical network deployment tasks adhere to the defined AWS network standards; * Execute implementation tasks following operational excellence standards and propose improvements in AWS DWDM network quality and reliability; * Design and implement scalable operational processes to sustain and expand our optical network infrastructure; * Lead end\-to\-end product evaluation and drive strategic commercial tender processes, ensuring optimal vendor selection and cost efficiency * Troubleshoot complex problems on optical networks and eventually propose innovative solutions to improve the deployment agility; * Lead and execute operational excellence programs that measurably improve DWDM network quality and reliability, reducing service\-impacting events and raising the bar on optical network performance; Our ideal candidate is highly autonomous, possesses strong written and verbal communication skills, strong project management and time management skills and has a successful track record of troubleshooting complex network problems and delivering innovative solutions. The desire and ability to work in a fast paced, collaborative environment is essential. This is an amazing opportunity in terms of responsibility, interesting challenges and high visibility. A day in the life This role oversees deployment of Metro and Long Haul systems and all processes related to those deliverables. It includes the design of the projects, generation of all technical documents, updating internal tools, deployment of systems in the real network and handover for operations. Also the deployment team is responsible to participate in testing new solutions and define technical requirements. Automation is a very important topic in this team to improve the deployment process, reduce the manual activities and make interaction between internal tools. **About The Team** Internet Edge provides high\-performing Internet connectivity for a wide range of use cases, such as retail (amazon.com), video streaming (Prime Video), content delivery (CloudFront), DNS (Route53\), AWS services (such as EC2/S3\), consumer devices (such as FireTV/Kindle/Echo), and more. Within AWS Networking, the Internet Edge organization is responsible for connecting Amazon to the global Internet. With connections to thousands of unique external networks in hundreds of locations across the world, our mission is to deliver an Internet connectivity experience that sets the global standard for performance, availability, security, and cost, making Amazon the clear choice for our customers’ needs. The Long Haul Deployment team is responsible for the installation and commissioning of the DWDM systems for Optical Backbone. Our mission is to deliver unconstrained inter\-regional transport at the lowest cost, with the most available product conceivable to AWS customers, making it invisible. The team vends capacity to internet backbone customers that provides low latency, high availability and supports features our customers expect. **About AWS** Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Information Technology, a related technical field or equivalent experience * 4\+ years' experience with major DWDM platforms; * 4\+ years' experience with optical planning, modelling tools and hands\-on implementation; * Experience with the rollout of large DWDM networks in Metro and Long Haul spaces; **Preferred Qualifications** * Knowledge of networking fundamentals * Knowledge of network design, protocols and Layer 1/2 troubleshooting * Experience with optical planning and modelling tools; and the rollout of large DWDM networks. * Optical network design, planning and implementation skills; Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. **Company** \- Amazon Development Centre Ireland Limited Job ID: A10427554

title: Outreach Operations Subject Matter Expert (With either Portuguese, French, German , Italian , Spanish or Arabic)
description: **Work Model –** 100% Office Based **Location** \- Eastpoint Business Park, Alfie Byrne Rd, East Wall, Dublin 3, D03 YK37 **Project Process Description** Outreach empowers one to create positive change for people and the planet with Geo tools. The project focuses on researching and outreaching to government and non\-government entities in various markets to collate geographic data for use on Google Maps through Google’s Geo Data Upload Tool. title: Part-Time Appeals Officers-Tribunal for Asylum and Returns Appeals (TARA)
description: **Job Title: Part\-Time Appeals Officers\-Tribunal for Asylum and Returns Appeals (TARA)** **Employer:** Department of Justice, Home Affairs and Migration **County:** Dublin **Closing Date:** 3pm, Thursday, 11th June 2026 **Starting Salary:** See Competition Information Booklet **Background:** A new international protection appeals structure, the Tribunal for Asylum and Returns Appeals (TARA), is to be established in 2026, to deal with international protection appeals, under the International Protection Act 2026 (2026 Act) which was signed into law on 22 April 2026\. The 2026 Act provides for new structures and processes to implement the EU Pact on Migration and Asylum (“The EU Pact”), a new framework to manage migration and asylum across Europe which intends to streamline international protection decisions. The 2026 Act will also deliver the Programme for Government commitment to ‘Replace the International Protection Appeals Tribunal with a new, faster appeals system’. TARA will replace the International Protection Appeals Tribunal (“IPAT”) which was established on the 31 December 2016 in accordance with section 61 of the International Protection Act 2015 (as amended). From June 2026 there will be a period during which both TARA and IPAT will be operating simultaneously and under separate legislative frameworks, with IPAT being wound down over time. TARA will process appeals arising under the 2026 Act, and IPAT will continue to process appeals arising under the International Protection Act 2015 (as amended). **Tribunal for Asylum and Returns Appeals (TARA)** TARA will be a statutorily independent body that exercises a quasi\-judicial function and is required to be operational by 11 June 2026\. It is expected to be able to process appeals in shorter timeframes than those which exist in the current system. The requirements of the EU Pact will bring about substantial reforms to the current international protection appeals process. These reforms include the introduction of various timelines for a decision to be made across different international protection application procedures, including for appeals, and the incorporation of appeal timeframes into national law. It will also give rise to the management of two new appeal types: * those against ‘return orders’ and, * decisions on the suspension of the duty to leave the State. Appeals received and managed under TARA are to be based on a blended model, where case workers (civil servants) support Part\-Time Appeals Officers in the decision\-making process. Caseworkers will set out facts of an appeal and carry out research at the request of the Part\-Time Appeals Officer. This approach is expected to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of the process for Part\-Time Appeals Officers in making a decision on an appeal, in meeting the requirements set out under the EU Pact and 2026 Act and the timeframes identified. **About the Role:** The Department of Justice, Home Affairs and Migration is now seeking to recruit a significant number of Part\-Time Appeals Officers. These roles present an opportunity for successful candidates to be part of a new and dynamic appeals process at a time of significant and fundamental change in the international protection system. Part\-Time Appeals Officer roles are statutory positions created under the International Protection Act 2026\. Successful candidates will be appointed by the Minister for Justice, Home Affairs and Migration (“the Minister”) as vacancies arise. All Appeals Officers must be able to deal with vulnerable persons, and persons with special procedural requirements, such as minors, unaccompanied minors, disabled people, elderly people, pregnant women, single parents with minor children, victims of human trafficking, persons with serious illnesses, persons with mental disorders and persons who have been subjected to torture, rape or other serious forms of psychological, physical or sexual violence, such as victims of female genital mutilation. In addition, they must be able to work with interpreters and research and assess relevant objective country information from reliable sources. **Please note** : Part\-Time Appeals Officers will be appointed for a term of seven years and may be re appointed for a second term not exceeding three years, though there is no guarantee of re\-appointment. **Key Functions of the Role** The 2026 Act sets out the functions of Part\-Time Appeals Officers which will include the following: * Ensuring that the business assigned to them is managed efficiently and disposed of as expeditiously as is consistent with fairness and natural justice; * Conducting oral hearings in accordance with the 2026 Act and relevant regulations made under the Act; * Have regard to any guidelines issued by the Chief Appeals Officer; • Preparing appeal reports as required and providing to the Chief Appeals Officer when requested to do so; * Attending any meetings convened by the Chief Appeals Officer; * Providing such assistance to the Chief Appeals Officer in the performance of their functions under this Act as the Chief Appeals Officer may reasonably request; * Complying with any direction given by the Chief Appeals Officer relating to training and the continued professional development of Appeals Officers. The Closing Date for this competition is Thursday, 11th of June at 3pm. We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act.

title: Partner Relationship Representative

title: People Consultant
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** One of the key drivers to our success is an exceptional emphasis on our people. We believe that our competitive advantage lies in our talent and that we can build a sustainable advantage by enabling individuals and teams to contribute their full potential. The People Consultant team helps to drive those efforts across our Tech, Core, and Business Organizations. This includes supporting our leaders and managers through scale, creating an environment where Stripes of all backgrounds can thrive, aligning our business strategy with our people strategy, using data to develop best practices from first principles. We are committed to building an inclusive work environment where all Stripes feel welcomed as their authentic selves—inclusive of all genders, sexual orientations, ethnicities, races, education, ages, and other personal characteristics. We work on broadening our internal diversity because we want to be culturally equipped to build products and solutions for our diverse user base. What you’ll do As a People Consultant, you’ll help make Stripe a great place to work and grow. You’ll work with business leaders, managers, and employees across Stripe to strategize and implement systems, processes, and programs to help people succeed. **Responsibilities** * Partner with and advise front line managers on performance management, coaching, and employee relations. Collaborate closely with Employee Relations and Legal teams to foster sound business decisions. * Work with the Legal team and external consultants to help ensure Stripe’s compliance with local labor regulations * Advise and consult front line management on Stripe People policies and best practices, in collaboration with fellow People team members and counsel as necessary * Partner directly with People Partners (HRBPs), managers, and leaders on wider organizational strategy and initiatives, including reorganizations * Facilitate smooth roll out of key People programs, including front line manager support for performance review cycles, compensation cycles, employee engagement surveys and ad hoc programs * Analyze and present on organizational health trends and insights to managers and leaders; manage tracking and reporting of some key people data to inform team strategy * Ensure excellent employee experience through key life cycle stages including the departure processes Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * Minimum of 3\+ years of HR experience; at least 1 year in a client facing HR role (e.g. HR Business Partner, Employee Relations partner) * Proven track record of partnering directly with managers on performance management, coaching, and resolving sensitive employee relations issues * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills demonstrated through success diagnosing problems using data, reasoning clearly and determining viable solutions, and remaining flexible in those solutions’ implementation * Experience utilizing company policies and practices to determine appropriate recommendations for action * Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience working in a fast paced, growing environment, preferably in a technology company or a large scale organization * Project management skills, with an ability to design and execute clear processes while successfully engaging peers and stakeholders to deliver work on schedule * Proven ability to identify areas for improvement and escalate recurring themes to key decision makers when needed In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €88,000 \- €132,000\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Privacy Associate (Dublin / London)
description: **Position Title: Privacy Associate (Dublin / London)** **Entity: Kroll Bond Rating Agency Europe Limited or Kroll Bond Rating Agency UK Limited** **Employment Type: Full\-time** **Location: Dublin, Ireland or London, England** **Summary/Overview** Kroll Bond Rating Agency Europe Limited/Kroll Bond Rating Agency UK Limited and its affiliates (“KBRA”) are seeking a Privacy Associate to join its Legal Department. You will be enthusiastic, highly motivated, have a strong work ethic, and be well\-equipped to work closely with other members of the Legal Department on privacy, data protection, and contract\-related matters. The position will be based out of our **Dublin or London office** . **About The Team** The Legal Department handles all of KBRA’s legal matters with the exception of ratings. The Legal Department works with analysts, compliance, finance, technology, and senior management across the company. **About The Job** As Privacy Associate, your responsibilities will primarily focus on privacy and data protection issues as well as contract drafting, reviewing, and negotiation. Specific responsibilities may include: * Advising on international data privacy and cybersecurity laws/regulations (including GDPR, UK GDPR, ePrivacy Directive, CCPA/CPRA, etc.). * Assisting in the enhancement, implementation, and ongoing maintenance of privacy, data protection, and cybersecurity policies and practices across KBRA. * Conducting and advising on privacy impact assessments, legitimate interest assessments and assisting with vendor and third\-party due diligence, and issues related to cross\-border data transfers. * Supporting records of processing activities. * Assisting with incident/ breach response, coordinating with Technology, Information Security, Legal, and Compliance and other internal stakeholders. * Drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts and clauses relevant to privacy, including data processing agreements. * Drafting, reviewing, and negotiating vendor and service provider contracts, client engagement letters, subscription agreements, and technology/service agreements. * Providing privacy‐related input into contract review workflows and advise internal teams on risk allocation and compliance. * Monitoring and tracking developments in privacy, data protection and AI, including enforcement, guidance updates, and standards in Ireland, UK, EU, and other relevant jurisdictions. * Reviewing and updating privacy notices and policies. * Keeping up to date with technology trends, including AI, and technology in use at KBRA and how they interact with privacy laws. * Liaising with external regulators, legal advisors, and auditors as needed. * Delivering training and awareness programs on privacy and data protection for internal teams. * Acting as a resource for internal teams with respect to privacy\-related and other legal questions. * Contributing to corporate governance on privacy matters, including preparing board or committee materials where required. * You will collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams (Compliance, Technology, Marketing, Business Development), as well as external counsel and regulators, as needed. **About You** You will be successful in this role if you have: * Bachelor’s degree in a related field required. * 4\-8 years post\-qualification experience, specifically with hands\-on exposure to privacy/data protection law. * Strong experience with drafting, negotiating, and reviewing contracts, especially with privacy\-sensitive clauses (DPAs, vendor agreements, client contracts, etc.). * Demonstrated knowledge of GDPR (EU \& UK), cross\-border data transfer mechanisms (e.g. Standard Contractual Clauses, Binding Corporate Rules, etc.), ePrivacy laws, vendor/third party risk, and cybersecurity basics. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Ability to explain legal and technical privacy concepts to non\-legal stakeholders. * Strong research, analytical, organisational, and project management skills, with great attention to detail. Able to manage multiple matters/tracks simultaneously under tight deadlines. * Experience working in regulated environments is an advantage (financial services, tech, data / analytics, etc.). * IAPP certification (CIPP/E, CIPP/US, CIPM, or equivalent) is a plus. **Benefits** * A flexible hybrid work schedule – Tuesdays, Wednesdays, Thursdays in the office * Competitive benefits and paid time off * Paid family and disability leave * Pension plan * Educational and professional development financial assistance * Employee referral bonus program \#HYBRID

title: Product Engineer
description: Healthcare is in crisis and the people behind the results deserve better. With more and more data coming from wearables, lab tests, and patient–doctor interactions, we’re entering an era where data is abundant. Junction is building the infrastructure layer for diagnostic healthcare, making patient data accessible, actionable, and automated across labs and devices. Our mission is simple but ambitious: use health data to unlock unprecedented insight into human health and disease. If you're passionate about how technology can supercharge healthcare, you’ll fit right in. Backed by **Creandum, Point Nine, 20VC, YC** , and leading angels, we’re working to solve one of the biggest challenges of our time: making healthcare personalized, proactive, and affordable. We’re already connecting millions and scaling fast. **Short on time?** * **You:** You’re an experienced product builder. You’re motivated to help predict chronic diseases \- faster and more affordably * **Us:** Team size 35, 6 engineers, revenue 4x'd in the last year * **Salary:** £90K \- £130K \+ equity * **Location:** Fully remote (GMT timezone) **Why we need you** Our small (for now) but mighty 6\-person engineering team is tackling big, ambitious problems across wearables and lab testing. We’re already: * Streaming time\-series data from 3M\+ wearables every second * Helping major digital health companies deliver 4M\+ lab tests across all 50 US states * Saving millions of patients thousands of dollars Now we’re aiming even higher: going from 4 million lab tests to 40 million diagnostics in the next year. To get there, we need full\-stack engineers who can help us build the next version of our diagnostics API. You don’t need healthcare experience but you do need to: * Get excited about shipping features that can cut medical bills by 10x * Be curious enough to unpack messy insurance incentives and parse thousands of records to estimate true out\-of\-pocket costs for patients * Ask sharp questions to take a problem from “rough idea” to shipped software * Stay pragmatic and comfortable navigating domain experts, constraints, and real\-world edge cases * Care deeply about UX: build interfaces that feel intuitive and clearly communicate complex healthcare information **What you’ll be doing day to day** * **Building our customer facing products:** we have dashboards for both product verticals, wearables and lab testing. For example, the lab testing dashboard allows customers to self serve and order lab tests for their patients. * **Speaking with customers:** for this role, we need you to be customer obsessed, asking thought\-provoking questions and digging into product usage * **Owning full\-stack features:** You’ll design and build new functionality end\-to\-end across frontend and backend. * **Tackling ambitious problems:** you’ll be breaking down technical challenges that haven’t been done before in healthcare, we need you to be extremely product minded with every line of code you write * **Iterating quickly:** ship, learn from real usage, and refine to make the experience better each time. **Requirements** * Startup engineering experience where you influenced what to build, not just how to build it. Bonus: you’ve built something from 0 → 1 * A track record of building and shipping full\-stack features end\-to\-end * Ability to turn messy or unclear problems into a clear plan * Comfortable working across frontend and backend (perfection in both not required) * Strong product judgment \- within a clear product direction * Focus on how people actually use the product, not just code elegance * Fast iteration based on feedback and metrics * Comfortable working in a domain with hard constraints and complex real\-world edge cases * Proactive about getting feedback from customers on what you ship *You don’t need to tick every box to fit in here. If the problems we’re solving genuinely interest you and you know you can contribute, we’d love to talk.* **How you'll be compensated** * Salary: £90K \- £130K \+ equity * Your salary is dependent on your location and experience level * Generous early stage options (extended exercise post 2 years employment) * Regular in\-person offsites, last were in Tenerife and Miami * Monthly learning budget of $300 for personal development and productivity * Flexible, remote\-first working \- including $1K for home office equipment * Monthly budget of $150 to use towards a coworking space * 25 days off a year \+ national holidays * Healthcare coverage depending on location **Oh and before we forget:** * Backend Stack: Python (FastAPI), Go, PostgreSQL, Google Cloud Platform (Cloud Run, GKE, Cloud BigTable, etc), Temporal Cloud * Frontend Stack: TypeScript, Next.js * API docs are here: https://docs.junction.com/ * Company handbook is here with engineering values \+ principles **Important details before applying:** * We only hire folks physically based in GMT and EST timezones \- more information here * We do not sponsor visas right now given our stage

title: Project Quantity Surveyor
company: GEDON™ | Commercial & Executive Search for the Built Environment
description: **Company Overview** Our client is a well\-established main contractor with a strong presence across both the Irish and UK construction markets. With an annual turnover comfortably in excess of €55m, they have built a reputation as a dependable and technically capable delivery partner across a broad range of building and civil works. The business is known for combining the agility of a mid\-tier contractor with the systems, governance and delivery standards of a larger organisation. They consistently deliver projects in the €10m to €70m range across both public and private sectors, with a portfolio spanning education, healthcare, civic, sports and other public infrastructure schemes. A key element of their recent growth has been their involvement in a strategic joint venture with a leading Tier 1 contractor, providing exposure to larger, more complex builds and reinforcing their position as a trusted delivery partner in the region. This continues to open up further opportunities across an expanding workload. With a clear long\-term growth plan and an increasingly strong order book, the business is investing in its commercial and project delivery teams. They place a real emphasis on developing their people, with structured progression pathways and a culture that supports those looking to advance their careers quickly. **Working Environment \& Projects** You will be joining a close\-knit commercial team working on a flagship sports development in the greater Dublin area, with the project programmed to run for approximately 24 months. The scheme represents a significant piece of work for the business and offers the chance to take genuine ownership of a high\-profile delivery from start to finish. The role is structured around a healthy balance of office and site\-based activity. The main office is located in Dublin, around 15 minutes from the project site, allowing for an efficient split between commercial reporting, contract administration and hands\-on site engagement throughout the working week. The emphasis sits firmly on the delivery phase rather than pre\-contract estimating, giving you the opportunity to play a leading role in driving the commercial performance of a live project. You will work closely with project managers, site teams, design consultants and the wider commercial function, gaining strong exposure across all aspects of project delivery. The wider environment is collaborative, supportive and performance\-led. The business invests in its commercial staff and rewards those who take initiative, with clear opportunities to grow into more senior commercial leadership roles as the workload continues to expand. **Responsibilities** * Take full commercial ownership of a flagship project from inception through to completion, with a strong focus on the delivery phase * Manage cost control, valuations, variations, change management and final accounts in line with company and contractual requirements * Prepare and issue monthly cost and value reconciliation reports, providing clear commercial visibility to senior management * Procure and manage subcontract packages, including tender analysis, negotiation, appointment and ongoing administration * Maintain strong working relationships with clients, design teams, subcontractors and the wider project team * Identify, manage and mitigate commercial risk throughout the project lifecycle * Ensure full compliance with contract terms, including the management of notices, claims and entitlements * Support and mentor junior commercial staff where appropriate, contributing to a strong team culture **What You Need** * Minimum 5 years’ post\-graduate experience in a Quantity Surveying role with a main contractor * Degree qualification in Quantity Surveying or a related construction discipline is preferred, though strong relevant experience will also be considered * A solid background delivering public sector projects – schools, healthcare, civic, justice or similar schemes – would be highly advantageous * Proven ability to manage projects in the €10m to €70m range from a commercial standpoint * Strong understanding of standard forms of contract and the broader commercial delivery lifecycle * Excellent communication, negotiation and stakeholder management skills * Self\-motivated, organised and comfortable taking ownership of your workload on a live, fast\-paced project **What You’ll Get in Return** A competitive base salary of up to €85,000 is on offer, supported by a pension contribution and wider benefits package. Beyond the package, this role provides a clear and realistic route to Senior Quantity Surveyor level within a business that is genuinely committed to developing its commercial team. You will play a central role on a flagship project, gain exposure to a varied and growing pipeline of work, and benefit from the stability of an established contractor with a strong order book on both sides of the Irish Sea. **How to Apply** To apply for this role, please send an up\-to\-date CV via the Apply Now button. For a confidential discussion, you can also contact **Finn Davey** on **\+353 1 270 9457** or email **finn@gedonexecutive.com** .

title: Quality Assurance Tech I
description: **Job Title: Quality Assurance Technician I** **Job Description** You will execute manual and automated test cases, validate cultural and linguistic accuracy, and identify, document, and track defects to resolution. Working within a defined test strategy and established Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), you will collaborate closely with QA Engineers, QA Managers, and cross\-functional teams to deliver reliable, natural, and culturally relevant voice interactions for customers. **Responsibilities** * Execute manual test cases accurately, following established SOPs and templates across existing components and features. * Perform cultural and linguistic validation to ensure responses are accurate, natural, and appropriate for the target locale, including verification of localisation elements such as date, time, units, and currency. * Assess linguistic aspects such as language fluency, dialect features, colloquialisms, speech fluidity, pitch, tone, and overall cultural relevance of responses. * Run automated test scripts, review automation failures, analyse root causes, and collect logs and traces to support further debugging. * Reproduce defect behaviour in components and features to validate issues and confirm that fixes are effective. * Perform defect verification to ensure resolved defects no longer reproduce across relevant environments, locales, and devices. * Create clear, well\-documented defect reports with detailed steps to reproduce, expected versus actual behaviour, logs, and screenshots. * Escalate appropriately when test scripts fail or when actions are required that deviate from established SOPs. * Analyse test results and provide clear, structured reporting on test execution status to stakeholders, partners, and other teams. * Install and configure software, tools, and test environments across multiple platforms, including Mac, Windows, Linux, and mobile devices, to support testing activities. * Recommend improvements to SOPs, tools, and metrics to increase the efficiency and effectiveness of testing processes. * Support regression testing by executing test plans across multiple locales, endpoints, and device types. * Collaborate closely with team members to remove blockers, share findings, and ensure timely delivery of high\-quality results. * Apply strong attention to detail and follow documented procedures precisely to maintain consistency and reliability in test outcomes. * Use Microsoft Office and similar productivity tools to document findings, prepare reports, and manage test\-related data. * Leverage research and analytical skills to investigate issues, interpret logs and error messages, and distinguish between product defects and environment\-related problems. **Essential Skills** * At least 2 years of experience in software quality assurance or software testing. * Proven experience executing manual and automated test cases in a structured testing environment. * Hands\-on experience with defect creation and management using bug tracking tools such as JIRA. * Ability to review automation failures, identify root causes, and differentiate between genuine defects and environment or data issues. * Ability to read and interpret logs, traces, and error messages for debugging and issue analysis. * experience installing and configuring software, tools, and test environments across Mac, Windows, Linux, and mobile devices. * Strong attention to detail with the ability to follow documented procedures and SOPs accurately and consistently. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English for documentation, reporting, and collaboration with distributed teams. * Technical aptitude and confidence working with software tools, test environments, and device configurations. * Self\-sufficiency in following SOPs, troubleshooting basic blockers, and escalating issues appropriately. * Proactive communication style, keeping stakeholders informed of progress, blockers, and test results without prompting. * Strong sense of ownership and responsibility for the quality and accuracy of test results. * Competence in using Microsoft Office or similar office productivity tools. * Solid data entry capabilities and research and analytical skills relevant to QA and testing work. **Additional Skills \& Qualifications** * Fluent proficiency in one or more non\-English languages, particularly Dutch, German, and/or French. * experience with cultural and linguistic validation, ensuring that content is appropriate, natural, and accurate for specific languages, dialects, and cultural contexts. * experience testing voice assistants, conversational AI, or products based on natural language understanding (NLU). * Familiarity with automation frameworks and the ability to run and review automated scripts, for example using Python, Ruby, or Shell scripts. * experience with internationalisation (i18n) and localisation testing across multiple languages and locales. * Exposure to CI/CD pipelines and a clear understanding of the Software Test Life Cycle (STLC). * experience working in Agile or Scrum environments, including participation in sprints and iterative delivery cycles. * Familiarity with smart home and IoT devices such as voice\-enabled speakers, streaming devices, and tablets, or similar consumer electronics products. * Interest in languages, social and cultural anthropology, consumer product internationalisation, and large language models. * Understanding of quality assurance and quality control principles, including quality management systems. * Knowledge of medical terminology where relevant to content or domain\-specific testing. * Demonstrated bias for action, taking initiative to unblock testing, pick up available work, and contribute to team goals. * Curiosity and a strong desire to learn new tools, technologies, and testing practices, continuously improving skills and processes. **Why Work Here?** You will join a collaborative, international team focused on creating high\-quality, culturally relevant customer experiences. The environment encourages proactive communication, ownership, and continuous learning, giving you the opportunity to deepen your technical skills in automation, AI, and localisation. You will work with modern tools and processes, including Agile methods and CI/CD, while contributing to widely used consumer technologies. The culture values curiosity, initiative, and self\-sufficiency, offering a supportive setting where your ideas for process and quality improvements are welcomed and acted upon. **Work Environment** The role sits within a structured quality assurance environment that follows defined test strategies, Standard Operating Procedures, and established templates. You will work closely with QA Engineers, QA Managers, and cross\-functional partners, often in an Agile or Scrum setting with iterative delivery cycles. Day\-to\-day work involves using a range of technologies and tools, including bug tracking systems such as JIRA, automation frameworks and scripts (for example in Python, Ruby, or Shell), and office productivity tools such as Microsoft Office. Testing covers multiple platforms and devices, including Mac, Windows, Linux, mobile devices, and smart home or IoT products. You will interact with CI/CD pipelines and modern software development practices, and you will regularly install an d configure software and test environments. The role requires careful documentation, clear reporting, and consistent adherence to SOPs in a professional, detail\-oriented setting Location Dublin, Ireland Trading as TEKsystems. Allegis Group Limited. Level 1, The Chase, Carmanhall Road, Sandyford, Dublin 18, Ireland. No. 909257\. TEKsystems is a company within the Allegis Group network of companies (collectively referred to as "Allegis Group"). Aerotek, Aston Carter, EASi, Talentis Solutions, TEKsystems, Stamford Consultants and The Stamford Group are Allegis Group brands. If you apply, your personal data will be processed as described in the Allegis Group Online Privacy Notice available at https://www.allegisgroup.com/en\-gb/privacy\-notices. To access our Online Privacy Notice, which explains what information we may collect, use, share, and store about you, and describes your rights and choices about this, please go to https://www.allegisgroup.com/en\-gb/privacy\-notices. We are part of a global network of companies and as a result, the personal data you provide will be shared within Allegis Group and transferred and processed outside the UK, Switzerland and European Economic Area subject to the protections described in the Allegis Group Online Privacy Notice. We store personal data in the UK, EEA, Switzerland and the USA. If you would like to exercise your privacy rights, please visit the "Contacting Us" section of our Online Privacy Notice at https://www.allegisgroup.com/en\-gb/privacy\-notices for details on how to contact us. To protect your privacy and security, we may take steps to verify your identity, such as a password and user ID if there is an account associated with your request, or identifying information such as your address or date of birth, before proceeding with your request. If you are resident in the UK, EEA or Switzerland, we will process any access request you make in accordance with our commitments under the UK Data Protection Act, EU\-U.S. Privacy Shield or the Swiss\-U.S. Privacy Shield.

title: Quantitative Campus Recruiter
description: **Overview** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. Our in\-house campus recruiting team is expanding! We are looking for a Quantitative Recruiter to join our quant recruiting team at our European headquarters in Dublin. If you are an individual who is passionate about mathematical problem solving and building social connections, then we want to hear from you! You will join a team of recruiters focused on hiring top\-tier talent into Susquehanna’s Quantitative Research and Quantitative Systematic Trading internships and grad programmes. Beyond interviewing and hiring, you will assist with the running of our summer internship and use data to refine our recruiting processes and strategies. We are looking for an innovative problem solver who can leverage new tools and ideas to strengthen our brand recognition, diversify our recruiting efforts, and attract the best talent. **What You’ll Do** * Leverage your mathematical background while interviewing and assessing quantitative campus candidates by working through probability, statistics, and logic questions. * Find creative ways to scale our campus efforts and attract top talent from universities across Europe. * Build and maintain strong relationships with candidates, hiring managers, and interviewers throughout the hiring process. * Attend and facilitate careers fairs, campus events, and on\-site events for our programmes. * Apply an analytical approach – leverage data to inform recruiting decisions and improve the hiring process. * Help onboard your hires and be involved in the running of the summer internship and graduate programmes. **What We're Looking For** * Bachelor’s degree in mathematics, engineering, physics, or computer science with coursework in probability, statistics, and combinatorics. * Experience with Excel preferred, but not essential. Basic programming experience also beneficial. * Strong appetite to learn how to interview students by using quantitative concepts. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Strong sense of urgency and emotional intelligence. * Strategic thinker with strong analytical skills. **What’s In It For You** Our culture enables you to drive meaningful impact, by leveraging your expertise and taking ownership. Private healthcare and onsite gym, plus wellness initiatives. Fully stocked kitchen, with daily breakfast and lunch. Regular social, sporting and community events including annual poker tournament. Matched donations and corporate discounts. Seasonal opportunities to travel for recruiting events in Ireland, the UK and mainland Europe. **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. **What We Do** Susquehanna’s deep integration of trading, technology and quant research makes us experts in trading essentially all listed financial products and asset classes, with a focus on derivatives. We handle millions of trading transactions around the world every day as both a market maker and market taker. Our efforts provide liquidity and ensure competitive prices for buyers and sellers. While our presence in the market is broad, our trading desks are highly specialized to allow for a deep understanding of the unique drivers of each asset class. *If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.*

title: Quantitative Data DevOps
description: **Overview** The Quantitative Engineering and Data group in Dublin is a new group focused on developing tools, processes and datasets to enable revenue generation using data at Susquehanna. We partner with Quantitative Researchers, Traders and other engineering teams in Dublin and across the globe. The QED Dublin group is on an exciting strategic mission that will transform the speed of innovation of the business. **Job Summary** You will work closely with the global engineering and DevOps resources to develop and operate the data processing systems that are a critical dependency for trading and quantitative research. The primary focus of the role is to improve the reliability and operational effectiveness of the data processing systems. You will also be critical in the productionisation of new datasets that unlock revenue for the business. title: Regional Marketing Specialist (French or German Speaking)
description: **At Poka, we’re transforming how frontline teams operate, learn, and solve problems.** **Our connected worker platform is purpose\-built for manufacturing, empowering frontline workers to share knowledge, collaborate in real\-time, and drive continuous improvement. Think of it as the missing digital link, bringing the human interface into the stack of the Smart Factory, generating the right data and insights that drive entirely new scales of process improvements.** **We’re proud to support global leaders like Nestlé, Bosch, Mars, and Danone in their digital transformation journeys and as part of the IFS family, our reach and impact continue to grow globally.** **Help us expand our presence across Europe** This is a great opportunity for a hands\-on marketer who loves building and executing campaigns that actually move the needle, pipeline, conversions, and everything in between. **About the role** We’re looking for a Regional Marketing Specialist, EMEA who will plan, orchestrate, and optimize multi\-channel campaigns across key European markets. You’ll work closely with regional \& global sales, product marketing, and digital teams to activate messaging that resonates with segment\-specific ICPs, fuels pipeline, and deepens engagement across all lifecycle stages. This role is ideal for someone with 2 \- 5 years of experience in B2B SaaS/technology environments who enjoys rolling up their sleeves, experimenting, and continuously improving performance. **What you’ll be doing** * Plan, execute, and optimize coordinated multi\-channel campaigns across email, paid social, search, webinars, events, and nurture workflows. * Build targeted campaigns for specific ICP segments across multiple regions (UK\&I, DACH, France, and more). * Drive measurable impact on pipeline metrics such as MQLs, SQLs, CPL, and Contributed Pipeline. * Support Account\-Based Marketing by creating tailored programs for priority accounts. * Manage campaigns in multi\-language environments (primarily English, German, and French), ensuring regional alignment and message consistency. * You will work in partnership with the local sales teams and act as the link between global marketing and sales. You will ensure regional marketing activity is aligned to the EMEA sales strategy and supports agreed priorities. * Support planning and execution of field events and regional activations. * Partner closely with sales to monitor performance, adjust targeting, and share insights. * Analyze campaign results, identify optimization opportunities, and report on performance with clarity and confidence. **What you bring** * 2 \- 5 years of experience in campaign execution, ideally within a B2B SaaS or technology environment. * Strong hands\-on experience running multi\-channel digital campaigns with measurable impact on funnel and pipeline performance. * Experience in planning and coordinating events, both digital and in\-person. * Familiarity with Account\-Based Marketing approaches and tools (e.g., 6sense, Demandbase, RollWorks). * Experience managing multi\-language campaigns (English required; German and/or French is required for regional delivery). * Confident building and optimizing email journeys, landing pages, and paid social/search campaigns. * Data\-driven mindset \- you instinctively ask “what’s working, why, and how do we scale it?” * Experience working closely with sales teams and aligning marketing activity to pipeline priorities. * Organized, collaborative, and comfortable owning campaigns from planning through to reporting. **Why Poka** * Join a fast\-growing global team redefining the future of frontline manufacturing workers. * Work in an environment where marketing experimentation is encouraged and supported. * Be part of a collaborative culture that invests in learning, innovation, and personal development. * Make a tangible impact \- your campaigns will directly shape our footprint across Europe. **What we offer:** * Dynamic and collaborative work environment. * Generous vacation policy upon arrival. * Remote work policy. * A truly flexible schedule that allows you to adapt your work hours. * Benefits Plan offered from day one. * MacBook Pro and €500 allowance for your home office. * Generous referral bonus. * The tools, support, and opportunities you need to keep learning and progressing in your career. *Want to learn more about our culture? Click HERE.* **Please note:** An understanding of English is essential at Poka, as it is the language used during company\-wide meetings and many internal communications, ensuring effective collaboration and decision\-making. In addition, as Poka operates in an international environment, English is the primary language for communicating with our clients and partners, enabling us to better serve them and represent Poka in a professional manner.
