title: Software Engineer | Sports Analytics | Experienced Hire
description: **Overview** As a Senior Software Developer at Susquehanna Sports, you will play a crucial role in designing, coding, and maintaining our next gen analysis and trading platform. We specialise in geopolitical and sports analytics across the globe. This is an opportunity to build out a next gen sports trading system within an early\-stage initiative focused on influential product development. Technology is at the forefront of everything we do at Susquehanna Sports, requiring cutting\-edge and innovative technology expertise. Our technologists at Susquehanna Sports enjoy working closely with quantitative researchers on highly complex in\-house software that runs on the most modern platforms available, solving a wide range of problems to help us grow. Susquehanna Sports is backed by our industry leading trading business and we’re introducing an initiative that will revolutionize the industry. Our people continue to grow and learn throughout their careers as market demands change the nature of our environment, thanks to our flat structure and collaborative environment. Join us and be part of a collaborative team that values continuous learning and growth. Are you passionate about sports wagering and trading? Do you thrive in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment where innovation is at the forefront? If so, we have an exciting opportunity for you! **What You'll Do** * Design and architect complex systems and software solutions from scratch * Build out an order sending system that will allow us to trade at scale, in many sports leagues across multiple exchanges * Integrate reference file and streaming data feeds from multiple vendors * Develop data recording and price evaluation tools * Deliver interactive presentations for business users and stakeholders * Participate in the full software development life cycle, including building, testing and production deployment **What We're Looking For** * Experience: proven success in server\-side software development, system design and technical event\-driven architecture * Skills: strong proficiency in C\# with previous Python experience and a solid understanding of Agile development methodologies * Experience with Kafka, Kubernetes and cloud computing is highly desired * Education: Bachelor's or Master's degree in computer science or a related STEM field or equivalent practical experience at the senior level * Interests: a keen interest in sports wagering, trading, and technology. Previous experience working within trading, sports analytics or sports wagering industries is highly preferred * Attributes: self\-motivated, detail\-oriented, excellent interpersonal and communication skills, and able to adapt to changing priorities **What We Offer** * Innovative environment: work on a mixture of highly complex in\-house software using the latest platforms * Growth opportunities: continuous learning and career development in a flat, collaborative structure * Mentorship: work with industry veterans and technical experts who are passionate about mentoring and sharing their knowledge * Perks: excellent salary, benefits, and opportunities to attend world sporting events * Team: join a small but growing team of skilled and passionate researchers, quants, and developers * Impact: have a direct impact on technology decisions and contribute to the growth of industry **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Solar & Renewable Energy Electrician
description: **Solar \& Renewable Energy Electrician** **€55,000 – €65,000 \+ Bonus \| Career Progression** Full‑Time, Permanent \| Leinster Region **Why Join My Power?** If you’re already working in solar or renewables and want **better organisation, higher quality installs, real progression and long‑term security**, this role is designed for you. * **€55,000 – €65,000 salary**, depending on experience \& certifications * **Performance bonuses** and annual salary review * **Funded training \& certifications** (Microgen, QC, advanced systems * Work with **high‑spec solar, battery, EV \& heat pump systems** * Stable pipeline of **domestic \& commercial projects** * Join a company genuinely driving **Ireland’s clean energy transition** **About My Power** My Power is a fast growing leader in Ireland’s solar PV and renewable energy sector. We deliver **high performance, compliant installations** for homes and businesses while maintaining a strong focus on **quality workmanship, safety and customer service**. Our reputation is built on doing the job properly, with the right systems, the right processes and the right people. As demand continues to grow, we’re expanding our senior electrical team to support projects across Leinster. **The Role** As a **Solar \& Renewable Energy Electrician**, you’ll take a role in the **installation, testing, commissioning and handover** of renewable energy systems. You’ll work with experienced project teams, support junior electricians and apprentices, and help maintain My Power’s high installation standards. This is a hands‑on role suited to an electrician who already understands **solar, batteries and modern electrical standards** and wants a **long‑term career path**, not just the next job. **Key Responsibilities** **Installation \& Commissioning** * Install, test and commission **Solar PV systems** in line with SEAI Code of Practice * Electrical integration of **string \& hybrid inverters, batteries, diverters, EV chargers and heat pumps** * Electrical upgrades to existing installations, where required * Carry out testing, inspection and certification to **IS 10101** and Safe Electric standards * Diagnose faults and support commissioning sign‑off * Complete test sheets, certifications, photographs and job documentation **Customer \& Site Engagement** * Deliver professional, clear system handovers to homeowners and site contacts * Explain system operation, protection, safety and aftercare * Represent My Power to the highest professional standard on site **Team \& Project Support** * Provide feedback to project managers on site conditions, materials and timelines * Participate in continuous improvement, quality and safety initiatives * Opportunity to progress into **Lead Installer / Supervisory roles** * Support and mentor apprentices and junior electricians **What We’re Looking For** * **Qualified Electrician** (National Craft Certificate or equivalent) * **Safe Electric registered** (or eligible to register) * **SEAI Microgeneration training** (or willingness to complete – supported) * **QC / Qualified Certifier** for testing \& verification (highly advantageous) * Experience with **solar PV, batteries and EV chargers** * Comfortable working at heights with a safety‑first mindset * Full, clean driving licence * Strong communication and leadership potential *We recognise experience matters, if you don’t tick every box but have strong renewables background, we still want to hear from you.* **Salary \& Benefits** * **€55,000 – €65,00 base salary** (DOE) * Performance‑based bonuses * Pension contribution * Annual salary review * Additional annual leave with service * Funded qualifications \& technical courses * Referral bonus **Apply Now** If you’re an **Electrician already working in renewables** and want **proper progression, quality installs and long‑term stability**, apply today. Pay: €55,000\.00\-€65,000\.00 per year Work Location: On the road

title: Solar Electrician
description: **About the Company** Join one of Ireland’s fastest‑growing solar energy providers. This organisation is known for high‑quality installations, strong safety standards, and a collaborative, people‑focused culture. As part of their growth, they are now recruiting qualified Solar Electricians to strengthen their installation teams. Ideally QC and SEAI but must have solar experience as a minimum. **Key Responsibilities** Solar PV Installation * Carry out the full electrical installation of Solar PV systems, including wiring panels, inverters, batteries, isolators, and associated equipment. * Ensure all work aligns with design specifications, manufacturer requirements, and national electrical regulations. Electrical Connections * Safely integrate solar systems into building electrical networks and the national grid. * Install and commission switchgear, breakers, meters, and protection devices to ensure seamless system operation. System Testing \& Commissioning * Perform comprehensive testing of completed systems, verifying voltages, currents, and performance metrics. * Diagnose and resolve wiring issues, faults, or configuration errors before handover. Compliance \& Safety * Follow all electrical safety standards, company procedures, and regulatory requirements. * Use PPE correctly and apply lockout/tagout procedures where required. * Ensure installations pass all inspections and meet grid‑connection compliance standards. Quality Workmanship * Deliver clean, organised, and professional electrical work. * Label circuits, tidy cabling, and maintain high visual and technical standards. * Document any design changes and communicate them to the engineering team. Maintenance \& Support * Conduct service visits, performance checks, and repairs on existing solar systems. * Troubleshoot underperformance or faults to minimise customer downtime. Team Collaboration * Work closely with roofers, installers, technicians, and site supervisors to keep projects on schedule. * Coordinate tasks efficiently and support apprentices or junior technicians when required. Customer Interaction * Represent the company professionally on\-site. * Explain basic system operation and answer general customer questions, escalating technical queries to project managers when needed. Continuous Improvement * Stay updated on new solar technologies, electrical standards, and installation techniques. * Participate in training and contribute ideas to improve safety, efficiency, and installation quality. **Required Qualifications** Trade Certification * Fully qualified electrician with a recognised certification (e.g., National Craft Certificate in Electrical Installation). * Current registration with **Safe Electric** (REC) or equivalent. Experience * Minimum **3 years’ experience** in electrical installation. * Solar PV experience strongly preferred (residential or commercial). Knowledge * Strong understanding of electrical systems, wiring methods, and national electrical regulations (e.g., IS 10101\). * Familiarity with PV components such as panels, inverters, and battery storage systems. Certifications * Safe Pass, Manual Handling, Working at Heights. * Additional solar‑specific training (SEAI, City \& Guilds, NABCEP) is an advantage. Driver’s Licence * Full, clean driving licence with willingness to travel to various project sites. Physical Requirements * Comfortable working on roofs, ladders, and in outdoor environments. * Ability to lift and handle solar equipment safely. **Key Competencies** * **Technical Proficiency:** Strong installation and troubleshooting skills; confident reading schematics and using electrical tools. * **Safety Focus:** Proactive in identifying hazards and maintaining a safe work environment. * **Attention to Detail:** High standards for workmanship, documentation, and system integrity. * **Problem Solving:** Able to adapt to unexpected site conditions and resolve technical challenges. * **Teamwork \& Communication:** Clear communicator, supportive team member, and capable mentor. * **Reliability:** Dependable, punctual, and committed to meeting project deadlines. * **Customer Service:** Professional, respectful, and focused on delivering a positive customer experience. Fill in the form, we will contact you...

title: Solutions Architect
description: **90% Remote Solutions Architect Role– Cloud \& Identity Transformation (€500/€550 Daily)** * **Design and deliver enterprise cloud \& identity solutions** * **Drive Azure transformation in a cloud\-first environment** * **12\-month contract and a hybrid working model** A leading reinsurance organisation is seeking an experienced Solutions Architect to join their enterprise IT architecture team at a pivotal point in their cloud transformation journey. This is a key role within a forward\-thinking environment that is actively modernising its core infrastructure and adopting a cloud\-first strategy across all services. The organisation is investing heavily in Azure and enterprise identity modernisation, with multiple programmes underway spanning cloud migration, security enhancement, and workplace transformation. You will play a central role in shaping the architecture that underpins these initiatives, working across highly complex, multi\-domain environments. This is a high\-impact contract role where you will influence architectural direction and guide large\-scale migration programmes. **Why you’ll love this role** * Lead enterprise architecture across Azure, Microsoft 365, and Identity platforms within a large\-scale cloud transformation programme * Take ownership of end\-to\-end architectural design decisions across major modernisation initiatives * Work within a cloud\-first organisation actively decommissioning legacy infrastructure at scale * Collaborate with a highly skilled architecture and engineering community with strong technical standards * Drive innovation across Intune, Azure AD, and Microsoft 365 strategy, including identity and security design * Enjoy a flexible hybrid model with 1–2 days onsite in Dublin and strong delivery autonomy **What you’ll be working on** * Azure cloud architecture design and governance across enterprise environments * Microsoft 365 migrations, including Exchange Online, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams * Identity and access management architecture using Active Directory and Azure Active Directory * Enterprise Mobility \& Security solutions, including Intune MDM/MAM, compliance, and threat protection * Cloud transformation and migration strategy for legacy on\-premise infrastructure * Security, compliance, and identity modernisation across multi\-domain environments **Key Requirements** * 4–5\+ years’ experience in a Solutions Architect or senior infrastructure architecture role * Strong expertise in Active Directory, Azure AD, and enterprise identity architecture * Proven experience with Enterprise Mobility \& Security (Intune MDM/MAM, information protection, threat protection, compliance tools) **Contract Terms** * Daily Rate: €500–€550 * Contract Length: 12 months (initial) * Location: Dublin (Hybrid – 1–2 days onsite per week) Contact Declan Ferrao on \+353 1902 6802 or send your CV to declan.ferrao@archer.ie

title: Sr. SDE, Amazon Email Services
description: **Description** We are looking for a seasoned Senior Software Development Engineer to join our Email Infrastructure \& Platform Engineering team. In this role, you will be a technical leader driving the design, development, and evolution of large\-scale distributed systems that power Amazon's global email infrastructure. You will work at the intersection of email services, AI\-powered automation, and platform reliability — contributing to one of Amazon's most critical internal infrastructure transformation initiatives. You will collaborate with cross\-functional teams across Email Platform, Security, IT Services, and AI/ML to deliver systems that are secure, scalable, and operationally excellent. Key job responsibilities Lead the design and development of highly available, fault\-tolerant distributed systems supporting Amazon's email routing, email distribution management, and email security infrastructure Drive technical strategy and architecture decisions for large\-scale service migrations and deprecations (e.g., legacy email systems to modern, API\-driven platforms) Build and integrate AI\-powered tooling and workflows to improve operational efficiency, including on\-call automation, anomaly detection, and intelligent routing Develop and maintain robust APIs and libraries consumed by internal teams, ensuring reliability, observability, and ease of use Partner with Security, Compliance, and Platform teams to implement perimeter security re\-architecture for inbound and outbound email flows Define and enforce engineering best practices including spec\-driven development, code quality standards, and operational excellence Mentor and grow junior engineers, providing technical guidance, code reviews, and career development support Contribute to org\-wide technical roadmaps and represent the team in cross\-organizational design reviews and planning A day in the life As an Sr. SDE on the AmazES Intelligence and Routing team, no two days look exactly the same — but every day is grounded in high\-impact engineering work that keeps Amazon's corporate email infrastructure running securely and intelligently for over 1 million Amazonians. You spend a meaningful portion of your time designing and building — writing production code, authoring technical specs, and making architectural decisions across our distributed email routing and API services. You invest equally in cross\-team collaboration, partnering with Amazon Security, IT Services, and AI teams to align on direction, resolve ambiguity, and drive complex initiatives forward. A significant part of your role is operational ownership — you care deeply about the health of the systems you build, improving observability, responding to incidents with rigor, and raising the reliability bar over time. And you consistently make time for people and culture — mentoring junior engineers, championing spec\-driven development, and helping shape a team environment that attracts and retains engineers who want to do the best work of their careers. **About The Team** AmazES Intelligence and Routing owns the intelligence and routing layer of Amazon's corporate email platform — the systems that classify, secure, and deliver email reliably at Amazon scale. We build and operate Farcaster, SEAL, BEAR, ELMO and many others working at the intersection of distributed infrastructure, email security, and applied AI. We value technical depth, spec\-driven development, and operational excellence — and we're transforming email into an intelligent, programmable platform for 1M\+ Amazonians. If you're excited about building systems that matter, working on hard problems at Amazon scale, and growing in an environment that rewards both technical excellence and customer obsession, we'd love to hear from you. **Basic Qualifications** * Experience as a mentor, tech lead or leading an engineering team * Experience leading the architecture and design (architecture, design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and current systems * Experience designing, building, operating, and managing large\-scale distributed systems or web services * Knowledge of concepts like system architecture, optimization, system dynamics, system analysis, statistical analysis, reliability analysis, and decision making * Experience with AWS services or other cloud offerings * Proficiency in one or more of: Java, Python, Go, or C\+\+ **Preferred Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent * Experience with full software development life cycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, and operations * Experience working with or evaluating AI systems * Knowledge of security practices like spam and phishing * Experience implementing cloud services including migrations and modernization projects or similar * Experience working across teams and influencing teams that are not your own * Experience in operational excellence, security compliance, and distributed systems resiliency * Experience with email protocols and infrastructure (SMTP, DKIM, SPF, DMARC, MTA configuration, email routing) Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. **Company** \- Amazon Development Centre Ireland Limited Job ID: A10426956

description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Customer Success Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. The role of the Success Guide is crucial in helping customers to grow value and improve adoption of Salesforce. Success Guides provide proactive and reactive architecture, product, and industry expertise to enable customers to successfully adopt products. The Success Guide will engage with customers across all account segments, delivering 1:1 success guidance to Premier and Signature Success customers. With the support of AI and automation, Success Guides provide the right recommendation at the right time, enabling customer value realization at scale. They partner with key collaborators such as Success Managers, Account Executives, and Support to make our customers successful. The ideal Success Guide is organized, agile, and passionate about Product Education. They have excellent presentation and communication skills in both a 1 on 1 and group setting. They have strong technical skills and are able to use their product knowledge to deliver value to both customers and internal teams. **Responsibilities** * Provide prescriptive recommendations, best practices, and technical advice for improving adoption * Deliver 1:1 Expert Coaching Sessions and Ad\-Hoc Success Engagements * Become an expert in Sales Cloud and related products * Help customers consistently achieve their business outcomes * Generate positive feedback from customers, internal teams and leadership * Show continued professional growth and development **Required Qualifications \& Skills** * 3\+ yrs relevant experience (Sales Cloud experience) * Undergraduate degree minimum * Trailhead Ranger and Salesforce Administrator Certified * Proven ability to quickly learn new technologies * Consultative and customer\-focused approach and engagement style * Ability to navigate, escalate, and lead efforts on complex customer requests * Demonstrated ability to communicate, present and influence credibly and effectively with a variety of audiences (from developers to C\-Suite) * Ability to prioritize, multi\-task, collaborate with team members and perform effectively under pressure **Preferred Qualifications \& Skills** * Trailhead Ranger * Salesforce Advanced Administrator Certified, Platform App Builder Certified, and Sales Cloud are considered an Asset * Familiarity with the Salesforce platform * Salesforce admin experience * Fluent in French, German, Italian, Spanish, or Portuguese In addition to having the Salesforce Administrator Certification, Success Guides will be expected to obtain Salesforce Advanced Administrator, Platform App Builder and Sales Cloud Consultant Certification within the first 90 days of hire. Expectation for candidate to enable and deliver on advanced topics such as Territory Planning, Maps and Platform related topics. title: Support Analyst
description: **Auxilia Group Recruitment are currently recruiting for a Support Analyst on behalf of our client operating in the technology industry. This is a permanent opportunity based in Dublin. The role will offer a hybrid working model.** **Role:** * Provide first and second line support for customer queries and incidents related to business applications, including ERP systems. * Act as a customer‑facing point of contact, ensuring professional, responsive, and service‑oriented experience at all times focused on calls first approach with performance troubleshooting and resolution. * Log, manage, and progress support tickets through to resolution using the company ticketing system, in line with SLAs and internal processes. * Perform initial triage, investigation, and troubleshooting of reported issues, and read to digest log files for investigation needs, provide escalation paths where appropriate, escalating where appropriate. * Work collaboratively with support team, consultants, and delivery teams to resolve more complex issues. **Requirements:** * 2\+ years’ experience in software or application support. * Qualification in IT, Computer Science or a related discipline. * Any prior exposure to ERP systems is an advantage. * Highly self‑motivated and proactive, with a strong appetite for continuous learning and self‑development. * Detail‑oriented with a methodical approach to troubleshooting and documentation. * Team‑focused, collaborative, and open to coaching and development.

title: Swedish Investment Counsellor
description: **Overview** Are you interested in working for an outstanding global investment firm and looking to take the next step in your career in Finance? Want to become an expert in and build a career advising clients on financial markets and investments? Want to focus on forging strong client relationships and providing exceptional client service? Fisher Investments Luxembourg – Dublin Branch is a European subsidiary of Fisher Investments, a privately\-held investment management firm that provides professional discretionary portfolio management services for high net worth private clients and some of the world's preeminent institutions. Our organisation has grown significantly over the past decade and we are looking for motivated candidates with finance backgrounds to join our team as Investment Counsellors to help us better the financial industry in Sweden as we continue our expansion in Europe. **The Opportunity:** The Investment Counsellor (IC) role is focused on managing relationships with high\-net\-worth clients in Sweden from our office in Dublin. You'll understand our clients' needs and educate them on the reasoning behind portfolio changes. This is crucial to your success in this role in maintaining long\-lasting relationships. You will report to your Team Manager who will aid in your professional development. **The Day\-to\-Day:** * Build long standing relationships with our clients, ensuring they always receive exceptional service * Manage a book of high\-net\-worth relationships with approximately €100\-150 million in assets * Be the conduit between our clients, our Investment Policy Committee, our research and trading departments, and several service teams * Proactively update clients on portfolio changes, financial markets, our current market strategy and investment philosophy * Gain advanced finance and capital markets education through our training program **Qualifications:** * 2\+ years exposure to private clients within the banking/wealth management industry * A sound knowledge of financial concepts, markets, current events and the market * Native Swedish and native or fluent English language skills (Proficiency in other European languages is beneficial) * Connect with and educate a variety of audiences * Maintain a positive personality and empathise with clients in good times and bad * MiFID certification, training or the equivalent * CFA or similar education is beneficial **Your Benefits:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Luxembourg align with our clients’ priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It’s the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks’ top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to €50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. * Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS LUXEMBOURG IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: System Administrator (Parttime or Fulltime)
description: **Overview** Trinseo is a different kind of global materials company – at the intersection of people, technology, and customers. We are a world leader in the production of plastics and latex binders. Our culture is built on passion and innovation. A career at Trinseo presents a unique opportunity to work in a highly collaborative environment. Helping customers solve their most complex material challenges is the reason we come to work each day. We are seeking innovative thinkers – ready to tackle any challenge and passionate about delivering value. As a global materials solutions provider focused on delivering innovative and sustainable solutions to our customers, we need people to feel respected and included so they can be more creative, innovative, and successful. We strive to create workplaces that reflect all communities and customers we serve and where everyone feels empowered to bring their full, authentic selves to work. That's why we're fully committed to inclusion across race, gender, age, religion, identity, and experience to drive us forward every day. Global Quality System Technologist is a gatekeeper of the Coding process. In this role the responsible employee is executing the Coding documentation process from the start through to full Material (GMID) Coding ensuring the right balance is met between high speed to market, customer satisfaction and diverse compliances. Correct Materials Management and Specification Management ensures that for new and/or adjusted Raw Materials and Products all the necessary steps are completed by all functions (Quality, R\&D, TS\&D, Supply Chain, Production, EH\&S, Accounting and Procurement) to buy raw materials and produce / sell products within the boundaries of legal compliance and (automotive) customer expectations. **Responsibilities** * Coordinate creation of new products and raw materials through the documentation processLead creation of new products and raw materials through the documentation process. * Create/Change/Discontinue Specifications and Recipes in SRR (Specification and Recipe Repository). * Perform the required transactions to link the SAP\-QM module to SRR and to LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) to ensure proper generation of Certificates of Analysis. * Create and maintain certificate profiles as well as quality information, following specification changes. * Coordinate with various functions to resolve bottlenecks in the Material documentation process . * Being responsible for timely and accurate entry of Material Master Data by providing the Business with the appropriate Material codes. * Facilitate the Material Code Request process for create, change, discontinue and reactivate requests. **Qualifications** * Being a flexible self\-starter, willingness to back\-up or assist other team members. * Detail oriented with high work process discipline. Pragmatic. * Experience with SAP and more specifically Quality System Architecture is a plus. * Strong written \& verbal English skills. * Effective communication and interpersonal skills being able to work cross\-functionally. * Ability to multi\-task and prioritize projects as appropriate according to committed deadlines. * Customer mindset. * Part\-time is possible.

title: Technical Customer Integration Engineer (d/f/m) - German Speaker
description: *Personio's intelligent HR platform helps small and medium\-sized organizations unlock the power of people by making complicated, time\-consuming tasks simple and efficient. Our team of 1,500 Personios is building user\-friendly products that delight our 15,000\+ customers and their 1\.5 million employees. Ready to make an impact from day one?* **The Role Introduction: “The Role”** Our **Customer Experience team** thrives on collaboration, working closely with teams across Personio to ensure that customer voices are at the heart of everything we do. We are dedicated to understanding and advocating for our customers, using data\-driven insights to shape our customer experience strategies and influence what we build. At Personio, integrations are the backbone of how our customers automate, scale, and unlock value from their HR data. As a **Sr. Customer Integration Engineer** , you'll design and deliver the technical connections that make Personio the central system of record for 15,000\+ customers. You'll sit at the intersection of technology, consulting, and innovation, helping customers realize value faster while evolving our integration frameworks and tools in collaboration with Product, Engineering, and Customer Success. **This position is office\-based (hybrid) in either Munich, Berlin or Dublin** **Role Responsibilities: “What You’ll Do”** * Integration Delivery: You partner with customers and internal teams to scope, design, and deliver seamless integrations between Personio and external systems (HRIS, payroll, identity, and productivity tools). * Technical Implementation: You build and troubleshoot API and SSO configurations, leveraging REST, GraphQL, SOAP, and webhook frameworks, while using automation platforms and AI\-powered tools to accelerate implementation. * Customer Advisory: You act as a trusted technical advisor, translating complex requirements into simple, actionable solutions that deliver measurable customer outcomes and improved time\-to\-value. * Knowledge Building: You contribute to documentation, tooling, and knowledge\-sharing that elevate the technical maturity of the Integration Services team. * Scalable Solutions (Senior): You develop and maintain reusable integration templates, SDKs, and connectors that accelerate delivery and improve consistency across the customer base. * Product Influence (Senior): You collaborate with Product and Engineering to identify integration trends, influence roadmap priorities, and improve platform capabilities. * Technical Leadership (Senior): You mentor colleagues on programming best practices and partner with the Integration Services Lead on technical decisions and standards. **Role Requirements: “What You’ll Bring”** * Working Experience: You have 2\-4 years (Professional) or 4\+ years (Senior) of relevant experience in customer\-facing roles in integration consulting or integration engineering. * Technical Foundation: You have a strong understanding of API ecosystems (REST, GraphQL, webhooks), data structures (JSON, XML), and working knowledge of at least one server\-side programming language (Python, Java, Node.js, Go, etc.). * Communication Excellence: You have exceptional communication skills in English and German (business fluent or higher), able to simplify technical detail for business audiences and build trust across teams and customers. * Growth Mindset: You're resilient, adaptable, and motivated to continuously improve how we deliver value, with a passion for learning and applying new technologies. * Cloud \& Architecture (Senior): You bring a basic conceptual understanding of DevOps concepts to support integration scalability and operational excellence. * Integration Tools (Nice\-to\-have): You have familiarity with iPaaS and automation platforms (Workato, Zapier, Make, etc.), cloud environments (AWS, GCP, Azure), or identity management standards (SSO, SAML, SCIM, OAuth). * AI \& Automation Curiosity (Nice\-to\-have): You're curious about AI and automation, from using AI tools for data mapping and testing to identifying new ways to accelerate customer onboarding. **Why Personio** Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values\-driven culture makes us stronger. We are proud to have an inclusive workplace environment that will foster your development no matter your gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race. At Personio, we value in\-person collaboration while also offering flexibility. This role is office\-based, with 3 days per week required in your contracted office location. The remaining days can be worked from home or in the office if you prefer. In addition, you’ll have 20 Flex Days per year to work remotely from other locations. Aside from our people, culture, and mission, check out some of the other benefits that make Personio a great place to work: * Receive a competitive reward package – reevaluated each year – that includes salary, benefits, and pre\-IPO equity * Enjoy 28 days of paid vacation, plus an additional day after 2 and 4 years * Make an impact on the environment and society with 1 (fully paid) Impact Day * Receive generous family leave, child support, mental health support, and sabbatical opportunities * We enjoy gathering for meals, cultural initiatives, and events like local Summer Sessions and year\-end celebrations. There's also healthy snacks, drinks, and a weekly catered lunch.

title: Technical Support Engineer
description: Job Title: Technical Support Engineer Location: Celbridge, Kildare Salary: €35,000 \- €40,000 DOE \+ benefits including health insurance, strong pension scheme and more Working Model: Hybrid following probation, flexible start/finish times Celtic Careers are working with a well\-established international engineering and technical services client looking to hire a Technical Support Engineer. Key Responsibilities • Provide day\-to\-day technical support to customers, field engineers, and internal teams • Manage customer queries professionally and efficiently via phone and email • Support spare parts identification, quotations, and service\-related requests • Assist service engineers with technical documentation and operational support • Coordinate with sales, engineering, and service teams on ongoing activities • Support remote monitoring and digital service platforms • Prepare technical reports, service documentation, and related records • Assist with customer site reviews, technical discussions, and service follow\-up activities • Identify opportunities to improve customer support and service delivery • Occasional travel to customer sites within Ireland may be required What We’re Looking For • Previous experience in a technical support, engineering support, or service coordination role • Strong customer service experience is essential • Confident communicator who is comfortable dealing directly with customers and stakeholders • Technical qualification or background in engineering, manufacturing, mechanical, utilities, pharma, or a related sector • Strong organisational and multitasking skills • Comfortable working across multiple teams in a fast\-moving environment • Experience supporting both internal and external customers preferred This role would suit someone who enjoys combining technical problem solving with customer interaction and is looking to build a long term career within a collaborative engineering and services environment. If you feel you would be a good fit for the role, please apply or email your CV to roisin@celticcareers.com *If you are living in Ireland and hold a VALID WORK PERMIT, we would love to hear from you, if however, you do not hold a valid work permit unfortunately we will not be in a position to assist you with your job search.* ***Celtic Careers is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer.***

title: Test Analyst
description: We are seeking an experienced IT Test Analyst to join a dynamic technology team responsible for delivering high\-quality digital solutions. The successful candidate will play a key role in ensuring that new systems, enhancements, and integrations meet defined business and technical standards. This is a hands\-on testing role requiring strong analytical ability, a structured approach to quality assurance, and a commitment to excellence. **Role Purpose** The IT Test Analyst will be responsible for planning, executing, and coordinating all phases of software testing across multiple projects. This includes developing test strategies, designing test cases, managing test environments, and collaborating closely with development and business teams to ensure successful delivery. **Core Responsibilities** * Analyse business and technical requirements to understand system functionality and testing needs. * Design and maintain comprehensive test plans, scripts, and scenarios to validate functionality, usability, performance, and security. * Conduct manual and automated testing activities to verify system behaviour and data integrity. * Manage test environments, datasets, and configurations to support accurate and repeatable test execution. * Identify, log, and track software defects through to resolution using appropriate tools and processes. * Provide clear, concise reporting on test outcomes, risks, and quality metrics to stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with developers, analysts, and project managers to resolve issues and enhance solution quality. * Support the continuous improvement of testing processes, tools, and documentation standards. **Experience \& Qualifications** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a software testing or QA role within a technology\-focused environment. * Relevant third\-level qualification in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. * Proven experience testing web, cloud, or enterprise applications. * Strong knowledge of software testing principles, tools, and methodologies. * Understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and common delivery approaches such as Agile or Waterfall. * Experience with defect management and test management tools (e.g., JIRA, Azure DevOps, TestRail). * Ability to create and maintain traceability between requirements, test cases, and results. * Excellent analytical, documentation, and communication skills. * Strong attention to detail and a commitment to producing high\-quality work. * Ability to work independently and contribute effectively within a team setting. **Desirable Attributes** * Professional certifications in software testing or quality assurance (e.g., ISTQB Foundation Level or equivalent). * Experience with test automation frameworks or scripting languages. * Exposure to API, integration, or performance testing. * Familiarity with DevOps and CI/CD environments. * Demonstrated initiative, curiosity, and a proactive approach to learning new tools and technologies. **Personal Qualities** * A structured thinker who enjoys problem\-solving and continuous improvement. * Excellent communicator who can translate technical concepts for non\-technical stakeholders. * Reliable, accountable, and focused on delivering results. * Adaptable and comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment with changing priorities.

title: Trade Surveillance Analyst
description: **Overview** We are seeking an experienced Trade Surveillance Analyst to join our EMEA Compliance team, based on the Dublin or London trading floor. This role focuses on market abuse surveillance and the enhancement of our surveillance framework. You will work closely with Trading, Technology, and Compliance teams to ensure our surveillance systems are robust, risk\-focused, and aligned with evolving regulatory expectations. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with strong market abuse experience who is keen to play an active role in shaping and improving surveillance controls within a fast\-paced, Trading environment. **In This Role, You Will** * Build a strong understanding of desk structures and trading strategies) to deliver risk\-based and commercially aware surveillance * Review and triage surveillance alerts, investigate potentially suspicious activity, and contribute to reporting of STORs where required * Develop, enhance, and update surveillance rules to improve effectiveness, reduce false positives, and ensure alignment with regulatory developments (e.g., MAR, MiFID II and related guidance) * Partner with Technology and Trading to prioritise and implement surveillance enhancements, including performing UAT and improving alert data quality and system logic * Support the ongoing maintenance of the surveillance framework, including documentation, system issue resolution, and cross\-jurisdictional coverage where applicable **What We're Looking For** **Experience \& Skills** * Approximately 3\+ years’ experience in trade surveillance, with a strong focus on market abuse; * Strong working knowledge of Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) and MiFID II **Desirable** * Experience investigating market abuse typologies (e.g., spoofing, layering, momentum ignition, insider dealing) * Experience in rule development, calibration, or optimisation within a surveillance system * Familiarity with listed products including derivatives * Experience working closely with trading teams * Experience with data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, SQL) **Competencies / Key Attributes** * Strong analytical skills with the ability to assess trading behaviour in context * Detail\-oriented with a structured and methodical approach to investigations * Strong written communication skills, particularly in drafting clear investigation summaries and regulatory reports * Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast\-paced trading environment * Collaborative team player with the confidence to engage constructively with traders, technology teams, the Compliance colleagues and senior stakeholders

title: Truck Driver (Category C license)
description: **Overview:** Our Drivers play a vital role in ensuring the safe, timely, and professional delivery of building materials to our customers and sites. Operating a Class C rigid vehicle, this role involves multi\-drop deliveries, vehicle safety checks, customer interaction, and compliance with all transport and health \& safety regulations. Reporting directly to our Yard Manager and working closely with the transport team and yard operatives, the driver is responsible for maintaining excellent service standards while always representing the company in a professional manner. **Key Responsibilities:** **Delivery Operations \& Route Management:** * Complete daily deliveries and collections safely, efficiently, and on schedule. * Adhere to assigned delivery routes while maintaining flexibility for changing customer and business requirements. * Ensure excellent route and customer knowledge across assigned delivery areas. * Carry out multi\-drop deliveries in a professional and timely manner. * Safely transport building materials while ensuring loads are secure and compliant with road safety regulations. * Assist with the safe loading and unloading of goods both on\-site and off\-site, ensuring materials are handled correctly and delivered without damage. * Support yard and warehouse teams with loading customer orders and supplier deliveries when required. * Ensure all loads are checked for accuracy, stability, and compliance with transport safety regulations prior to departure. **Vehicle Maintenance \& Compliance:** * Conduct daily walkaround vehicle inspections and report defects immediately to the Transport Supervisor or relevant manager. * Maintain vehicles in a clean, roadworthy, and legally compliant condition at all times. * Operate tachograph systems correctly and maintain accurate driving records. * Complete all required delivery dockets, PODs (Proofs of Delivery), invoices, and transport paperwork accurately and on time. * Report any vehicle incidents, damages, or delivery issues promptly. * Check and report any defects relating to vehicles, loads, or products. **Customer Service \& Communication:** * Deliver excellent customer service during all deliveries and collections. * Build and maintain strong working relationships with customers, suppliers, and colleagues. * Represent the company professionally while on customer sites and public roads. * Communicate effectively with transport managers, yard staff, and management regarding delivery updates or delays. **Health \& Safety:** * Adhere to all company Health \& Safety procedures and current legislation. * Ensure safe loading, unloading, and handling of materials at all times. * Wear appropriate PPE while carrying out duties. * Follow all site\-specific safety requirements when attending customer locations. * Assist in maintaining a safe and organised working environment. **Expectations:** As a Driver, you are expected to show: **Professionalism:** Maintain a professional appearance and positive attitude while representing the company. **Reliability \& Flexibility:** Demonstrate punctuality, dependability, title: UKI Business Consulting - Life Sciences, SC/AM
description: Location: Dublin 2 Other locations: Primary Location Only Salary: Competitive Date: 22 May 2026 **Job description** ------------------- Requisition ID: 1711818 **Business Consulting \- Transformation Architecture, Senior Consultant / Assistant Manager** **General Information** **Location:** Dublin / Cork **Available for Visa Sponsorship:** No **Business Area:** Business Consulting **Contract Type:** Full\-Time – Permanent **The Team** At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. EY believes a better working world means addressing big, complex issues to deliver outcomes that grow, optimise and protect our clients' businesses. The world is constantly changing, creating abundant opportunities and essential risks for government, public and private sector organisations. In Transformation Architecture, we help our clients see their future and define their purpose, and then we design, lead and deliver the end\-to\-end transformations to realise this ambition. **The opportunity** Life Sciences is a rapidly growing sector in EY Ireland. We are expanding our Life Sciences Strategy and Transformation capability, with a focus across the end‑to‑end pharmaceutical, biotech and medtech value chain \- including R\&D, Medical Affairs, Commercial, Market Access and health system engagement. As a Senior Consultant, you will help deliver high‑quality engagements for Life Sciences clients, applying analytical rigour, strategic insight and creative problem‑solving. You will work alongside senior team members to support the design and delivery of innovative solutions that address affordability, access, operational and patient‑outcome challenges across health systems and industry. This is the ideal opportunity for individuals with strong consulting experience \- or experience within a Life Sciences organisation \- who are eager to accelerate their career in Life Sciences business consulting and contribute to the growth of EY’s Life Sciences practice in Ireland. **Your key responsibilities** As a Senior Consultant in Transformation Architecture, you will; * Be focused on delivery of challenging transformation projects, supporting clients across the Life Sciences value chain, applying critical thinking to help address complex challenges for pharmaceutical, biotech and medtech organisations. * Contribute to engagement workstreams focused on areas such as disease area strategy, asset positioning, launch readiness, Market Access and pricing, medical affairs excellence, and patient pathway optimisation. * Conduct qualitative and quantitative research, synthesising insights into clear, compelling outputs aligned to client objectives. * Support senior team members in the preparation and facilitation of client workshops, stakeholder interviews and working sessions. * Develop high‑quality deliverables including frameworks, reports, models and strategic recommendations. * Support internal initiatives to strengthen EY’s Life Sciences capability, sector offerings and thought leadership. * Collaborate effectively within multidisciplinary teams across EY’s Consulting practice. **To qualify for the role you must have** * At least 3 years’ experience with a consulting firm or in industry within a pharmaceutical, biotech or medtech organisation. * Experience supporting projects in areas such as Commercial Strategy, Market Access, Pricing, Medical Affairs, evidence generation, RWE, Digital Health, New Product Planning or related domains. * A strong academic background, ideally in a Life Sciences, Health, Economics, Business or related discipline. * Experience in client‑facing environments with exposure to stakeholder engagement and the development of structured recommendations. **Ideally, you’ll also have some experience of delivering elements of projects such as:** * A postgraduate degree in a relevant discipline (e.g., MSc, MPH, MBA, Health Economics, Pharmaceutical Sciences). * Experience working across geographies or with global Life Sciences clients. * Exposure to thought‑leadership development (e.g., contributing to articles, white papers or presentations). * A willingness to travel internationally as required. **What we look for** We are seeking individuals who are intellectually curious, passionate about Life Sciences and motivated to help clients identify innovative and implementable solutions. You will thrive in this role if you enjoy working in a fast‑paced environment, collaborating across multidisciplinary teams and contributing to EY’s growing Life Sciences community. **What working at EY offers** We offer a competitive remuneration package. Our comprehensive benefits package includes support for flexible working and career development, and with FlexEY you can select benefits that suit your needs: covering holidays, health and well\-being, insurance, savings and a wide range of discounts, offers and promotions. Plus, we offer: * Support and coaching from some of the most engaging colleagues around. * Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career. * The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you. All our employees are given a benefits package which they can tailor to suit their individual preferences. Our range of benefits include: * Hybrid Working * Pension/Discounted Health Insurance * Web Doctor * Purchase of Additional Annual Leave * Free Gym Membership * Travel Pass * Maternity \& Paternity Leave * Bike to Work Scheme * Referral Bonuses \& Recognition Awards * Tech MBA paid by EY EY is committed to being an inclusive employer and we are happy to consider flexible working arrangements. We strive to achieve the right balance for our people, enabling us to deliver excellent client service whilst allowing you to build your career without sacrificing your personal priorities. While our client\-facing professionals can be required to travel regularly, and at times be based at client sites, our flexible working arrangements can help you to achieve a lifestyle balance. **Career Progression** * When you join EY, you will be supported to ensure that you are enhancing your skills from day one. * Continuous learning, where you can develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next. * As you grow and develop here, you’ll discover opportunities to help customise your career journey, so that it’s as unique as you are \- success is defined by you, we will provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way. * Transformative leadership, we will give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs. * Diverse and inclusive culture, you will be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs. * We have embraced Hybrid working at EY adding greater flexibility and autonomy to the roles of our employees. **About EY** As a global leader in assurance, tax, transaction and advisory services, we’re using the finance products, expertise and systems we’ve developed to build a better working world. That starts with a culture that believes in giving you the training, opportunities and creative freedom to make things better. Whenever you join, however long you stay, the exceptional EY experience lasts a lifetime. **Inclusion \& Diversity** We hold a collective commitment to foster an environment where all differences are valued and respected, practices are equitable and everyone experiences a sense of belonging: Inclusion, diversity, and equity are part of who we are at EY. We believe that the highest\-performing teams maximize the power of different perspectives and backgrounds. These teams are both diverse and inclusive and are willing to invite and learn from other perspectives. Our ability to include various viewpoints into our mindsets, behaviours and operations is fundamental to driving innovation, building strong relationships, and delivering the best solutions for our clients. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and building a culture where we support all our people to achieve their potential. You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process. **If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.** **Join us in building a better working world. Unsolicited CVs / Profiles supplied to EY by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role.**

title: User Researcher – Product Strategy
description: ****Summary**** Love solving sticky problems? Excited about crafting innovative solutions for global software? Interested in working on products that touch everyone’s lives? We’re a close\-knit and multidisciplinary UX team working to make cloud software that is at the forefront of the insurance industry. We are committed to inclusion and diversity in skills and talent, as diverse as the global clients we serve. Our success is dependent on building teams that include people from divergent backgrounds and experiences who will support and challenge each other's assumptions with fresh perspectives. Our team tackles a variety of domains within Property and Casualty insurance; as a design (UX) researcher you would be in charge of guiding, organizing, and executing research. Working with support from our Lead Design Researcher, you will help our UX designers and product managers in Dublin and Poland to determine when and how the research will help them make better business and design decisions. We are looking for someone who is located in EMEA, self\-motivated, self\-directing, and interested in growing as a strategic problem\-solver on our friendly, global, customer\-centric UX team. ****Job Description**** **Your day\-to\-day:** * Help user experience teams and the business solidify research objectives and identify problems to be explored. Then, translate these objectives into key research questions for study and/or exploration. * Work across teams to plan and conduct an array of practical and impactful design research to uncover user attitudes, behaviors, needs, and motivations. * Understand when and which quantitative and qualitative research methods to use and their pros and cons. Guide teams on which research tradeoffs are acceptable given research questions, business goals, timeline, and resources. * Design and execute end\-to\-end custom, primary research. This includes but is not limited to building study plans, participant screeners, and discussion guides as well as recruiting study participants, moderation research sessions, and generating reports. * Uncover useful findings and actionable insights that drive tactical and strategic product decisions. Use third\-party, market research, analytics, and user research to generate solid insights to foster solutions that consider a holistic view of people, technology, and business. * Cultivate user empathy by translating research into compelling stories of how users of our solutions think, feel, and behave. * Build artifacts such as personas, journey maps, presentations, reports, etc. to communicate the needs of users and customers to key stakeholders in clear and memorable ways. * Assist with coaching and training developers, designers, product managers, and others on basic research best practices, enabling them to quickly turn\-around meaningful, useful insights. * Help to build and maintain an archive of research activities, results, and insights that can easily be shared with product shareholders and the user experience team. * Understand the importance of scaling user research and assist with developing and maturing research at Guidewire. ****What You Need To Be Successful:**** * Proven proficiency in common qualitative and quantitative research methods such as moderated and unmoderated usability studies, customer and user interviews, concept testing, cognitive walkthroughs, surveys, heuristic evaluations and expert reviews, and benchmarking. * Experience in conducting both generative and evaluative research. * Experience working with designers, developers, and product partners to define research objectives, identify and prioritize research. * Ability to draw out insights from a variety of design research artifacts. * Experience with crafting UX artifacts like personas, storyboards, and journey maps to illustrate user needs and areas for opportunity. * Ability to communicate results and opportunities in clear, concise, and compelling ways that fuel ideation and elevated designs. * Ability to prioritize and manage numerous projects simultaneously. * Flexibility to quickly respond to emerging issues under tight deadlines and lack of available end users. * Strong organization and time management skills. * Ability to work well in a team and collaborate with others. * Demonstrated ability to embrace AI and apply it to your current role as well as data\-driven insights to drive innovation, productivity, and continuous improvement. * Willingness to work across time zones \- We have teams from California to Poland, so you should be comfortable working across those time zones. ****Qualifications:**** * Bachelor’s, Master’s, or Ph.D. with 3 – 5 years of experience in Human Factors, Human\-Computer Interaction, Psychology, Cognitive Science, Design, UX Research or related fields or equivalent practical experience. * Passion for research and empathy for the user * Experience conducting international research (Ideal but not required) * Strong verbal and written English communication skills ****How We Work At Guidewire**** * Collaboration: We build on each other’s ideas and draw on interdisciplinary approaches to craft impactful experiences. * Integrity: We stand behind our words and commitments as part of a global community, respecting and recognizing our interdependence. * Curiosity: We are lifelong learners, with a growth mindset, who are passionate about advancing our discipline through entertaining new ideas and unique perspectives. * Humility: We leverage our expertise while respecting other viewpoints and challenging our own assumptions. ****About Guidewire**** Guidewire serves a global community of more than 350 insurers of all sizes and lines of business in more than 30 countries. Community feedback enables us to stay at the forefront of what is happening all over the world in P\&C and to incorporate insights into the design of our platform to be truly customer centric. Guidewire exists to deliver the industry platform that P\&C insurers rely upon to adapt and succeed in a time of accelerating change—and to ensure that every customer succeeds in the journey. We provide the software, services, and partner ecosystem to enable our customers to run, differentiate, and grow their business. Guidewire Insurance Platform is the P\&C industry platform that unifies software, services, and partner ecosystem to power our customers’ business. Insurance Platform provides the standard upon which insurers can engage their customers, optimize their operations, drive smart decisions, and innovate quickly. We are privileged to serve more than 450 P\&C insurers in 32 countries. We invest heavily in R\&D to build a technology platform that combines three elements—core processing, data and analytics, and digital engagement—to enhance insurers’ ability to engage and empower their customers and employees. Their primary areas of focus are to ensure alignment of that architecture with business objectives and high\-level company technology direction. ****About Guidewire**** Guidewire is the platform P\&C insurers trust to engage, innovate, and grow efficiently. We combine digital, core, analytics, and AI to deliver our platform as a cloud service. More than 540\+ insurers in 40 countries, from new ventures to the largest and most complex in the world, run on Guidewire. As a partner to our customers, we continually evolve to enable their success. We are proud of our unparalleled implementation track record with 1600\+ successful projects, supported by the largest R\&D team and partner ecosystem in the industry. Our Marketplace provides hundreds of applications that accelerate integration, localization, and innovation. For more information, please visit www.guidewire.com and follow us on Twitter: @Guidewire\_PandC. Guidewire Software, Inc. is proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We are committed to an inclusive workplace, and believe that a diversity of perspectives, abilities, and cultures is a key to our success. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship, marital status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability. All offers are contingent upon passing a criminal history and other background checks where it's applicable to the position.

title: Water Engineer
description: **Senior Civil Engineer (Water \& Drainage)** We are seeking a motivated and forward\-thinking Senior Civil Engineer with strong expertise in water and drainage infrastructure. This role suits an experienced engineer who excels at problem\-solving, delivering technically robust solutions, and mentoring others in a collaborative environment. A keen interest in sustainable urban drainage, water infrastructure, and integrated civil design is essential, as you will play a key role in delivering projects that enhance resilience, environmental performance, and the public realm while supporting the continued growth of our practice. **About the Company** Clifton Scannell Emerson Associates (CSEA) is a long\-established Civil, Structural and Transportation Engineering consultancy with over 70 years’ experience delivering high\-quality design and project management services across Ireland and internationally. We are trusted on major infrastructure and building projects, combining technical excellence with practical, buildable solutions. As an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited Employer, we are committed to continuous professional development, mentoring, and career progression within a collaborative, team\-focused environment where people are supported to grow and succeed. Since 2010, CSEA has been at the forefront of Data Centre design, delivering projects across Ireland, the UK, and mainland Europe. We have successfully implemented Building Information Modelling (BIM) from concept through to handover on a vast number of projects. Currently, CSEA employs over 115 staff across our two offices. **About the Role** Lead the delivery of civil engineering design with a primary focus on water, wastewater, and drainage infrastructure across a range of projects, including urban development, active travel schemes, and public realm improvements. **Responsibilities** * Take responsibility for designs through all project stages — feasibility, preliminary, detailed design, and construction support. * Design and develop sustainable drainage systems (SuDS), stormwater networks, foul drainage, attenuation systems, watermain infrastructure, and integrated road/drainage solutions. * Prepare and review drawings, calculations, hydraulic models, and technical deliverables in accordance with relevant Irish and European
